---
title: "Cost of service by heating subclass (NY statewide and per-utility)"
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

This notebook builds delivery **cost of service**, **revenue**, and **cross-subsidy** tables by `heating_type_v2` subclass for each of the seven New York utilities and for the state as a whole.

**Data source:** master BAT datasets on S3 under `s3://data.sb/switchbox/cairo/outputs/hp_rates/ny/all_utilities/<batch>/run_<delivery>+<supply>/cross_subsidization_BAT_values/`. These decompose each building's annual electric bill into delivery vs supply and carry BAT metrics, marginal-cost burdens, and residual components.

The notebook is **multi-scenario**: a `SCENARIOS` registry at the top defines each proposed-run variant (rate design, residual allocation method). The baseline comparison is always **run 1+2** (default uniform rates). Each scenario gets its own section with the full set of statewide + per-utility comparison tables.

## Setup

```{python}
#| label: setup

from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path

import numpy as np
import polars as pl
import yaml
from great_tables import GT, loc, style

from lib.great_tables import SB_GT_PER_YEAR_HTML_PATTERN, get_switchbox_gt_tab_options
from lib.quarto import display_gt
from lib.rdp import fetch_rdp_file, parse_urdb_json

report_vars: dict = {}
```

## Parameters

```{python}
#| label: params

BATCH = "ny_20260416a_r1-36"
STATE_LOWER = "ny"
RDP_REF = "205041d"
S3_BASE = "s3://data.sb/switchbox/cairo/outputs/hp_rates"

RUN_DEFAULT = "1+2"
PATH_MASTER_BAT_DEFAULT = (
    f"{S3_BASE}/{STATE_LOWER}/all_utilities/{BATCH}/run_{RUN_DEFAULT}"
    "/cross_subsidization_BAT_values/"
)

# Local ResStock `_sb` release (monthly load curves, NY); must match `build_master_bat` upgrade logic.
PATH_RESSTOCK_NY_LOAD_CURVE_MONTHLY = Path(
    "/ebs/data/nrel/resstock/res_2024_amy2018_2_sb/load_curve_monthly/state=NY"
)
PATH_COS_NOTEBOOK_CACHE = Path("../cache")

UTILITIES = ["cenhud", "coned", "nimo", "nyseg", "or", "psegli", "rge"]
UTIL_DISPLAY: dict[str, str] = {
    "cenhud": "Central Hudson",
    "coned": "Con Edison",
    "nimo": "National Grid",
    "nyseg": "NYSEG",
    "or": "O&R",
    "psegli": "PSEG-LI",
    "rge": "RG&E",
}

HT_V2_ORDER = (
    "heat_pump",
    "electrical_resistance",
    "natgas",
    "delivered_fuels",
    "other",
)
HT_V2_LABELS: dict[str, str] = {
    "heat_pump": "Heat pump",
    "electrical_resistance": "Electric resistance",
    "natgas": "Natural gas",
    "delivered_fuels": "Delivered fuels",
    "other": "Other",
}
```

## Scenario registry

Each entry defines a proposed-run variant: the CAIRO run pair, the residual allocation method used in BAT column selection, the tariff name fragments (for extracting volumetric rates into table subtitles), and a human-readable label. Every scenario is rendered as its own section of comparison tables below.

The EPMC entries (`SCENARIOS[1]`, `SCENARIOS[3]`) are retained here to preserve positional indices used by downstream cells (`SCENARIOS[0]`, `[2]`, `[4]`), but the entire EPMC section below is currently disabled via `#| eval: false` because the EPMC-calibrated tariffs for some utilities are not available at the pinned `RDP_REF`. Do not remove these entries without first refactoring all `SCENARIOS[<int>]` lookups to be id-keyed.

```{python}
#| label: scenarios

SCENARIOS: list[dict] = [
    {
        "id": "hp_seasonal_percustomer",
        "run": "5+6",
        "residual_alloc": "percustomer",
        "hp_tariff": "hp_seasonal",
        "nonhp_tariff": "nonhp_default",
        "label": "HP seasonal rate, per-customer residual",
        "description": (
            "HP customers on seasonal delivery rate; non-HP on recalibrated "
            "flat. Residual allocated per customer."
        ),
    },
    {
        "id": "hp_seasonal_epmc",
        "run": "21+22",
        "residual_alloc": "epmc",
        "hp_tariff": "hp_seasonal_epmc",
        "nonhp_tariff": "nonhp_default",
        "label": "HP seasonal rate, EPMC residual",
        "description": (
            "HP customers on seasonal delivery rate; non-HP on recalibrated "
            "flat. Residual allocated via EPMC."
        ),
    },
    {
        "id": "hp_seasonalTOU_flex_percustomer",
        "run": "13+14",
        "residual_alloc": "percustomer",
        "hp_tariff": "hp_seasonalTOU_flex",
        "nonhp_tariff": "nonhp_default",
        "label": "HP seasonal TOU flex rate, per-customer residual",
        "description": (
            "HP customers on seasonal TOU flex delivery rate; non-HP on "
            "recalibrated flat. Residual allocated per customer."
        ),
    },
    {
        "id": "hp_seasonalTOU_flex_epmc",
        "run": "25+26",
        "residual_alloc": "epmc",
        "hp_tariff": "hp_seasonalTOU_flex_epmc",
        "nonhp_tariff": "nonhp_default",
        "label": "HP seasonal TOU flex rate, EPMC residual",
        "description": (
            "HP customers on seasonal TOU flex delivery rate; non-HP on "
            "recalibrated flat. Residual allocated via EPMC."
        ),
    },
    # EH seasonal applies to HP *and* ER customers jointly; the
    # ``hp_tariff``/``nonhp_tariff`` keys are inherited from the HP-focused
    # scenarios above and repurposed here (``hp_tariff`` holds the tariff
    # applied to all electric-heating customers; ``nonhp_tariff`` holds the
    # tariff applied to everyone else).
    {
        "id": "eh_seasonal_percustomer",
        "run": "33+34",
        "residual_alloc": "percustomer",
        "hp_tariff": "elec_heat_seasonal_percustomer",
        "nonhp_tariff": "non_electric_heating",
        "label": "EH seasonal rate, per-customer residual",
        "description": (
            "HP + ER customers on electric-heating seasonal delivery rate; "
            "fossil-fuel customers on recalibrated flat. Residual allocated "
            "per customer."
        ),
    },
]
```

## Shared functions

All reusable logic lives here so per-scenario sections stay small.

```{python}
#| label: shared-functions

import uuid

_COS_GT_TAB_OPTS = get_switchbox_gt_tab_options()
_RDP_TARIFF_DIR = f"rate_design/hp_rates/{STATE_LOWER}/config/tariffs/electric"

# Matches ``table_body_hlines_color`` in ``get_switchbox_gt_tab_options`` (between-spanners divider only).
_COS_CMP_SPANNER_GAP_COLOR = "#D8D8D8"


def _cos_gt_comparison_table_css(
    table_id: str,
    *,
    tbody_nth_after_first_spanner: int,
    thead_leaf_nth_end_first_spanner: int,
) -> list[str]:
    """Default-vs-proposed GT tables: solid black top rule, thinner spanner underlines, gray column dividers.

    ``tbody_nth_after_first_spanner`` — last cell index of the first spanner in each body row (stub=1,
    COS=2, then first-spanner ``td``s). Totals 6, averages 4.

    ``thead_leaf_nth_end_first_spanner`` — in the **leaf** header row only (stub/COS are ``rowspan=2``,
    so they have no cells there), ``nth-child`` of the last ``th`` under the first spanner — that cell
    needs ``border-right`` or the gray rule between spanners has a gap between the spanner row and
    ``tbody``. Totals 4, averages 2.
    """
    tid = table_id
    c = _COS_CMP_SPANNER_GAP_COLOR
    n = tbody_nth_after_first_spanner
    leaf_n = thead_leaf_nth_end_first_spanner
    return [
        f"#{tid} thead tr.gt_spanner_row > th {{ "
        f"border-top: 3px solid #000000 !important; border-top-color: #000000 !important; }}",
        f"#{tid} .gt_column_spanner {{ "
        f"border-bottom-width: 1px !important; border-bottom-style: solid !important; "
        f"border-bottom-color: #000000 !important; }}",
        f"#{tid} thead tr.gt_spanner_row > th:nth-child(2) {{ "
        f"border-right: 1px solid {c} !important; }}",
        f"#{tid} thead tr.gt_spanner_row > th:nth-child(3) {{ "
        f"border-right: 1px solid {c} !important; }}",
        f"#{tid} thead tr.gt_col_headings:not(.gt_spanner_row) > th:nth-child({leaf_n}) {{ "
        f"border-right: 1px solid {c} !important; }}",
        f"#{tid} tbody tr > *:nth-child(2) {{ border-right: 1px solid {c} !important; }}",
        f"#{tid} tbody tr > *:nth-child({n}) {{ border-right: 1px solid {c} !important; }}",
    ]


def _cos_gt_tab_options_comparison(
    *,
    table_width: str,
    table_id: str,
    tbody_nth_after_first_spanner: int,
    thead_leaf_nth_end_first_spanner: int,
) -> dict[str, object]:
    return get_switchbox_gt_tab_options(
        table_layout="auto",
        table_width=table_width,
        extra_table_additional_css=_cos_gt_comparison_table_css(
            table_id,
            tbody_nth_after_first_spanner=tbody_nth_after_first_spanner,
            thead_leaf_nth_end_first_spanner=thead_leaf_nth_end_first_spanner,
        ),
    )


def _cos_gt_center_cells(gt: GT, *, data_columns: list[str]) -> GT:
    return (
        gt.cols_align(align="center", columns=data_columns)
        .tab_style(
            style=style.text(align="center"),
            locations=loc.stub(),
        )
        .tab_style(
            style=style.text(align="center"),
            locations=loc.column_labels(),
        )
    )


def _fmt_usd_0(x: float) -> str:
    return f"${x:,.0f}"


def _fmt_usd_total_title(x: float) -> str:
    """Format large dollar totals for table titles (e.g. ``$169 million`` instead of full precision)."""
    ax = abs(x)
    if ax >= 1_000_000:
        m = int(ax // 1_000_000)
        return f"${m} million"
    return _fmt_usd_0(ax)


_KWH_PER_GWH = 1_000_000.0


def _is_missing_gt_cell(x: object) -> bool:
    if x is None or isinstance(x, str):
        return True
    try:
        v = float(x)
    except (TypeError, ValueError):
        return True
    return v != v  # nan


def _snap_cos_tbl_cross_subsidy_display(cos_tbl: pl.DataFrame) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Snap float dust on totals so GT does not show −$0 / −0.0% (e.g. All customers row)."""
    out = cos_tbl.with_columns(
        pl.when(pl.col("cross_subsidy").abs() < 0.5)
        .then(0.0)
        .otherwise(pl.col("cross_subsidy"))
        .alias("cross_subsidy"),
    )
    if "cost_of_service" not in out.columns or "pct_overpayment_vs_cos" not in out.columns:
        return out
    return out.with_columns(
        pl.when(pl.col("cost_of_service") > 0)
        .then(pl.col("cross_subsidy") / pl.col("cost_of_service"))
        .otherwise(None)
        .alias("pct_overpayment_vs_cos"),
    )


def _snap_comparison_totals_cross_subsidy_display(df: pl.DataFrame) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Same snapping for default-vs-proposed totals (cross-subsidy and ratio columns)."""
    out = (
        df.with_columns(
            pl.when(pl.col("xsub_default").abs() < 0.5)
            .then(0.0)
            .otherwise(pl.col("xsub_default"))
            .alias("xsub_default"),
            pl.when(pl.col("xsub_proposed").abs() < 0.5)
            .then(0.0)
            .otherwise(pl.col("xsub_proposed"))
            .alias("xsub_proposed"),
        )
        .with_columns(
            pl.when(pl.col("cost_of_service") > 0)
            .then(pl.col("xsub_proposed") / pl.col("cost_of_service"))
            .otherwise(None)
            .alias("pct_xsub_cos_proposed"),
        )
        .with_columns(
            pl.when(pl.col("pct_xsub_cos_default").abs() < 0.0005)
            .then(0.0)
            .otherwise(pl.col("pct_xsub_cos_default"))
            .alias("pct_xsub_cos_default"),
            pl.when(pl.col("pct_xsub_cos_proposed").abs() < 0.0005)
            .then(0.0)
            .otherwise(pl.col("pct_xsub_cos_proposed"))
            .alias("pct_xsub_cos_proposed"),
        )
    )
    if "delta_rev_delivery" in out.columns:
        out = out.with_columns(
            pl.when(pl.col("delta_rev_delivery").abs() < 0.5)
            .then(0.0)
            .otherwise(pl.col("delta_rev_delivery"))
            .alias("delta_rev_delivery"),
        )
    return out


def _fmt_total_kwh_as_gwh(x: object) -> str:
    """Weighted total annual kWh as whole GWh (values stored in kWh in the frame).

    Suffix uses the same muted `` / year`` span as currency columns (``SB_GT_PER_YEAR_HTML_PATTERN``).
    """
    if _is_missing_gt_cell(x):
        return "—"
    gwh = float(x) / _KWH_PER_GWH
    return f"{gwh:,.0f} GWh<span class=\"sb-gt-per-year-suffix\"> / year</span>"


def _fmt_avg_kwh_per_customer(x: object) -> str:
    """Weighted mean annual kWh per customer-year (values stored in kWh in the frame).

    ``kWh`` stays body-colored; `` / year`` uses the muted span class (same as ``fmt_currency`` pattern).
    """
    if _is_missing_gt_cell(x):
        return "—"
    return (
        f"{float(x):,.0f} kWh"
        '<span class="sb-gt-per-year-suffix"> / year</span>'
    )


def cos_sw_summary_abridged_title(cos_tbl: pl.DataFrame) -> str:
    """One-line headline from the Heat pump row: % of customers vs % of delivery revenue."""
    _hp = cos_tbl.filter(pl.col("subclass") == "Heat pump")
    if _hp.height != 1:
        msg = "Expected exactly one 'Heat pump' row for statewide summary title"
        raise ValueError(msg)
    _pct_cust = float(_hp["pct_customers_display"][0]) * 100
    _pct_rev = float(_hp["pct_all_revenue"][0]) * 100
    return (
        f"Residential electric customers with heat pumps make up {_pct_cust:.1f}% of customers but pay "
        f"{_pct_rev:.1f}% of delivery revenue"
    )


def cos_table_baseline_source_note(*, residual: str | None = None) -> str:
    base = (
        "Total customer count from 2024 EIA-861, subclass customer counts estimated "
        "using RECS 2020. Building loads and utility assignments from ResStock. "
        "Cost-of-service from Switchbox analysis."
    )
    if residual == "epmc":
        return (
            base
            + " Assumes equal-percentage-of-marginal-cost (EPMC) residual allocation."
        )
    return base


def cos_baseline_totals_title(util_label: str | None, cos_tbl: pl.DataFrame) -> str:
    _hp = cos_tbl.filter(pl.col("subclass") == "Heat pump")
    if _hp.is_empty():
        amt = "—"
    else:
        amt = _fmt_usd_total_title(float(_hp["cross_subsidy"][0]))
    if util_label is not None:
        return (
            f"{util_label}'s electric customers with heat pumps are subsidizing those who heat with fossil fuels by {amt} a year"
        )
    return (
        f"New York State's electric customers with heat pumps are subsidizing those who heat with fossil fuels by {amt} a year"
    )


def cos_baseline_avg_title(util_label: str | None, cos_tbl: pl.DataFrame) -> str:
    _hp = cos_tbl.filter(pl.col("subclass") == "Heat pump")
    if _hp.is_empty() or float(_hp["n_customers"][0]) <= 0:
        avg_s = "—"
    else:
        avg = float(_hp["cross_subsidy"][0]) / float(_hp["n_customers"][0])
        avg_s = _fmt_usd_0(avg)
    if util_label is not None:
        return (
            f"{util_label}'s heat pump customers are overpaying by an average of {avg_s} per year"
        )
    return f"New York State's heat pump customers are overpaying by an average of {avg_s} per year"


# Keep in sync with ``utils/post/build_master_bat.py`` (UPGRADE_00_RUNS / UPGRADE_02_RUNS).
# Includes 25 for ``25+26`` EPMC TOU flex runs (same ResStock upgrade as 21/22).
# Includes 29, 30, 33, 34 for EH seasonal runs (upgrade 00, pre-HP); 31, 32, 35, 36 post-HP.
_RESSTOCK_UPGRADE_00_RUNS = frozenset({1, 2, 5, 6, 9, 10, 13, 14, 17, 18, 21, 22, 25, 29, 30, 33, 34})
_RESSTOCK_UPGRADE_02_RUNS = frozenset({3, 4, 7, 8, 11, 12, 15, 16, 19, 20, 23, 24, 31, 32, 35, 36})

RESSTOCK_ELEC_TOTAL_KWH_COL = "out.electricity.total.energy_consumption"
RESSTOCK_ELEC_NET_KWH_COL = "out.electricity.net.energy_consumption"


def resstock_parquet_upgrade_for_run_pair(run_pair: str) -> str:
    """Map a CAIRO ``run_*`` pair (e.g. ``1+2``, ``5+6``) to ResStock ``upgrade=`` parquet folder."""
    r = int(str(run_pair).split("+")[0].strip())
    if r in _RESSTOCK_UPGRADE_00_RUNS:
        return "00"
    if r in _RESSTOCK_UPGRADE_02_RUNS:
        return "02"
    msg = f"No ResStock upgrade mapping for delivery run {r} (pair {run_pair!r})."
    raise ValueError(msg)


def load_ny_annual_elec_kwh_by_bldg(
    path_state_monthly: Path,
    upgrade: str,
    *,
    path_cache: Path | None = None,
) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Sum monthly ``out.electricity.total.energy_consumption`` to annual kWh per ``bldg_id``."""
    if path_cache is not None and path_cache.exists():
        return pl.read_parquet(path_cache)
    udir = path_state_monthly / f"upgrade={upgrade}"
    if not udir.exists():
        msg = f"ResStock load curves not found: {udir}"
        raise FileNotFoundError(msg)
    elec = RESSTOCK_ELEC_TOTAL_KWH_COL
    out = (
        pl.scan_parquet(str(udir / "*.parquet"))
        .select(["bldg_id", elec])
        .group_by("bldg_id")
        .agg(pl.col(elec).sum().alias("annual_elec_kwh"))
        .collect()
    )
    if path_cache is not None:
        path_cache.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        out.write_parquet(path_cache)
    return out


def join_resstock_annual_elec_kwh(
    bat: pl.DataFrame,
    *,
    path_state_monthly: Path,
    run_pair: str,
    path_cache_dir: Path,
) -> pl.DataFrame:
    upgrade = resstock_parquet_upgrade_for_run_pair(run_pair)
    cache = path_cache_dir / f"resstock_ny_annual_elec_kwh_u{upgrade}.parquet"
    kwh = load_ny_annual_elec_kwh_by_bldg(
        path_state_monthly, upgrade, path_cache=cache
    )
    return bat.join(kwh, on="bldg_id", how="left")


def load_ny_annual_elec_net_kwh_by_bldg(
    path_state_monthly: Path,
    upgrade: str,
    *,
    path_cache: Path | None = None,
) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Sum monthly ``out.electricity.net.energy_consumption`` to annual net kWh per ``bldg_id``."""
    if path_cache is not None and path_cache.exists():
        return pl.read_parquet(path_cache)
    udir = path_state_monthly / f"upgrade={upgrade}"
    if not udir.exists():
        msg = f"ResStock load curves not found: {udir}"
        raise FileNotFoundError(msg)
    elec = RESSTOCK_ELEC_NET_KWH_COL
    out = (
        pl.scan_parquet(str(udir / "*.parquet"))
        .select(["bldg_id", elec])
        .group_by("bldg_id")
        .agg(pl.col(elec).sum().alias("annual_elec_kwh_net"))
        .collect()
    )
    if path_cache is not None:
        path_cache.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        out.write_parquet(path_cache)
    return out


def join_resstock_annual_elec_net_kwh(
    bat: pl.DataFrame,
    *,
    path_state_monthly: Path,
    run_pair: str,
    path_cache_dir: Path,
) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Join annual **net** electricity kWh from ResStock monthly load curves."""
    upgrade = resstock_parquet_upgrade_for_run_pair(run_pair)
    cache = path_cache_dir / f"resstock_ny_annual_elec_net_kwh_u{upgrade}.parquet"
    kwh = load_ny_annual_elec_net_kwh_by_bldg(
        path_state_monthly, upgrade, path_cache=cache
    )
    return bat.join(kwh, on="bldg_id", how="left")


def _bat_col_name(base: str, residual_alloc: str) -> str:
    """Build the actual parquet column name for a given allocation method.

    BAT columns are always qualified: ``BAT_{alloc}_delivery``.
    Residual share columns use the unqualified ``residual_share_delivery``
    for per-customer (the default) and ``residual_share_epmc_delivery``
    for EPMC.
    """
    if base == "BAT":
        return f"BAT_{residual_alloc}_delivery"
    if base == "residual_share":
        if residual_alloc == "percustomer":
            return "residual_share_delivery"
        return f"residual_share_{residual_alloc}_delivery"
    msg = f"Unknown base column: {base}"
    raise ValueError(msg)


def _bat_col_name_supply(base: str, residual_alloc: str) -> str:
    """Parquet column names for supply-side BAT and residual share."""
    if base == "BAT":
        return f"BAT_{residual_alloc}_supply"
    if base == "residual_share":
        if residual_alloc == "percustomer":
            return "residual_share_supply"
        return f"residual_share_{residual_alloc}_supply"
    msg = f"Unknown base column: {base}"
    raise ValueError(msg)


def load_master_bat(
    path: str,
    residual_alloc: str,
    *,
    utility: str | None = None,
) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Load master BAT, select the right columns for the allocation method, and rename to generic names.

    When ``utility`` is None, returns all utilities (for statewide tables).
    """
    bat_col = _bat_col_name("BAT", residual_alloc)
    res_col = _bat_col_name("residual_share", residual_alloc)

    lf = pl.scan_parquet(path, hive_partitioning=True)
    if utility is not None:
        lf = lf.filter(pl.col("sb.electric_utility") == utility)

    return (
        lf.select(
            "bldg_id",
            "weight",
            "sb.electric_utility",
            "postprocess_group.heating_type_v2",
            "annual_bill_delivery",
            "economic_burden_delivery",
            pl.col(res_col).alias("residual_delivery"),
            pl.col(bat_col).alias("bat_delivery"),
        )
        .with_columns(
            (pl.col("economic_burden_delivery") + pl.col("residual_delivery")).alias(
                "cost_of_service_delivery"
            ),
        )
        .collect()
    )


def load_master_bat_supply(
    path: str,
    residual_alloc: str,
    *,
    utility: str | None = None,
) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Load master BAT supply columns; rename to same schema as ``load_master_bat`` for ``build_cos_table``."""
    bat_col = _bat_col_name_supply("BAT", residual_alloc)

    lf = pl.scan_parquet(path, hive_partitioning=True)
    if utility is not None:
        lf = lf.filter(pl.col("sb.electric_utility") == utility)

    if residual_alloc == "vol":
        # No residual_share_vol_supply column in the parquet; derive from identity:
        # residual = bill - economic_burden - BAT
        return (
            lf.select(
                "bldg_id",
                "weight",
                "sb.electric_utility",
                "postprocess_group.heating_type_v2",
                pl.col("annual_bill_supply").alias("annual_bill_delivery"),
                pl.col("economic_burden_supply").alias("economic_burden_delivery"),
                pl.col(bat_col).alias("bat_delivery"),
            )
            .with_columns(
                (
                    pl.col("annual_bill_delivery")
                    - pl.col("economic_burden_delivery")
                    - pl.col("bat_delivery")
                ).alias("residual_delivery"),
            )
            .with_columns(
                (pl.col("economic_burden_delivery") + pl.col("residual_delivery")).alias(
                    "cost_of_service_delivery"
                ),
            )
            .collect()
        )

    res_col = _bat_col_name_supply("residual_share", residual_alloc)
    return (
        lf.select(
            "bldg_id",
            "weight",
            "sb.electric_utility",
            "postprocess_group.heating_type_v2",
            pl.col("annual_bill_supply").alias("annual_bill_delivery"),
            pl.col("economic_burden_supply").alias("economic_burden_delivery"),
            pl.col(res_col).alias("residual_delivery"),
            pl.col(bat_col).alias("bat_delivery"),
        )
        .with_columns(
            (pl.col("economic_burden_delivery") + pl.col("residual_delivery")).alias(
                "cost_of_service_delivery"
            ),
        )
        .collect()
    )


def estimate_volumetric_supply_bat(
    bat_pc: pl.DataFrame,
    kwh_col: str = "annual_elec_kwh",
) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Re-allocate per-customer supply residual to volumetric, per utility.

    Takes the already-loaded per-customer supply BAT DataFrame and redistributes
    each utility's total residual pool proportionally to ``kwh_col``.  This never
    reads ``BAT_vol_supply`` — it is an independent estimate for cross-checking.

    CAIRO's volumetric BAT uses ``electricity_net`` (net of PV) from hourly load
    curves.  To approximate that, pass a net-kWh column rather than the default
    total-consumption column.
    """
    util_stats = bat_pc.group_by("sb.electric_utility").agg(
        (pl.col("weight") * pl.col("residual_delivery")).sum().alias("_total_residual_u"),
        (pl.col("weight") * pl.col(kwh_col)).sum().alias("_total_kwh_u"),
    )
    return (
        bat_pc.join(util_stats, on="sb.electric_utility", how="left")
        .with_columns(
            pl.when(pl.col("_total_kwh_u") > 0)
            .then(pl.col(kwh_col) / pl.col("_total_kwh_u") * pl.col("_total_residual_u"))
            .otherwise(pl.col("residual_delivery"))
            .alias("residual_delivery"),
        )
        .with_columns(
            (pl.col("economic_burden_delivery") + pl.col("residual_delivery")).alias(
                "cost_of_service_delivery"
            ),
            (
                pl.col("annual_bill_delivery")
                - pl.col("economic_burden_delivery")
                - pl.col("residual_delivery")
            ).alias("bat_delivery"),
        )
        .drop("_total_residual_u", "_total_kwh_u")
    )


def assert_bill_identity(bat: pl.DataFrame, scenario: str) -> None:
    _check = bat.with_columns(
        (
            pl.col("economic_burden_delivery")
            + pl.col("residual_delivery")
            + pl.col("bat_delivery")
            - pl.col("annual_bill_delivery")
        )
        .abs()
        .alias("bill_identity_abs_err"),
    )
    _mx = float(_check["bill_identity_abs_err"].max())  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    assert _mx < 1e-2, (
        f"{scenario}: annual_bill_delivery should equal EB + residual + BAT "
        f"(max err {_mx})"
    )


def build_cos_table(
    bat: pl.DataFrame,
    *,
    rev_req: float,
    utility_filter: str | None = None,
) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Aggregate BAT data into a COS table by heating_type_v2.

    ``rev_req`` is the total delivery revenue requirement for the scope (utility or statewide).
    When ``utility_filter`` is not None, filters to that utility first.

    Expects ``annual_elec_kwh`` on ``bat`` (from ResStock load curves); missing column uses zeros.
    """
    df = bat
    if utility_filter is not None:
        df = bat.filter(pl.col("sb.electric_utility") == utility_filter)

    if "annual_elec_kwh" not in df.columns:
        df = df.with_columns(pl.lit(0.0).alias("annual_elec_kwh"))
    else:
        df = df.with_columns(pl.col("annual_elec_kwh").fill_null(0.0))

    total_w = float(df["weight"].sum())
    total_rev = float((df["weight"] * df["annual_bill_delivery"]).sum())
    total_kwh = float((df["weight"] * df["annual_elec_kwh"]).sum())
    display_customer_total = int(round(total_w))

    _by_ht = df.group_by("postprocess_group.heating_type_v2").agg(
        pl.col("weight").sum().alias("n_customers"),
        (pl.col("weight") * pl.col("annual_bill_delivery"))
        .sum()
        .alias("revenue_delivery"),
        (pl.col("weight") * pl.col("cost_of_service_delivery"))
        .sum()
        .alias("cost_of_service"),
        (pl.col("weight") * pl.col("bat_delivery")).sum().alias("cross_subsidy"),
        (pl.col("weight") * pl.col("annual_elec_kwh")).sum().alias("kwh_total"),
    )

    _spine = pl.DataFrame({"postprocess_group.heating_type_v2": list(HT_V2_ORDER)})
    _agg_cols = [
        "subclass",
        "n_customers",
        "revenue_delivery",
        "cost_of_service",
        "cross_subsidy",
        "kwh_total",
    ]
    by_ht = (
        _spine.join(_by_ht, on="postprocess_group.heating_type_v2", how="left")
        .with_columns(
            pl.col("n_customers").fill_null(0.0),
            pl.col("revenue_delivery").fill_null(0.0),
            pl.col("cost_of_service").fill_null(0.0),
            pl.col("cross_subsidy").fill_null(0.0),
            pl.col("kwh_total").fill_null(0.0),
        )
        .with_columns(
            pl.col("postprocess_group.heating_type_v2")
            .replace(HT_V2_LABELS)
            .alias("subclass"),
        )
        .drop("postprocess_group.heating_type_v2")
        .select(_agg_cols)
    )

    _total = pl.DataFrame(
        {
            "subclass": ["All customers"],
            "n_customers": [float(by_ht["n_customers"].sum())],
            "revenue_delivery": [float(by_ht["revenue_delivery"].sum())],
            "cost_of_service": [float(by_ht["cost_of_service"].sum())],
            "cross_subsidy": [float(by_ht["cross_subsidy"].sum())],
            "kwh_total": [float(by_ht["kwh_total"].sum())],
        }
    ).select(_agg_cols)

    cos_tbl = (
        pl.concat([by_ht, _total], how="vertical")
        .with_columns(
            (pl.col("n_customers") / total_w).alias("pct_all_customers"),
            (pl.col("revenue_delivery") / total_rev).alias("pct_all_revenue"),
            (pl.col("cost_of_service") / rev_req).alias("pct_revenue_requirement"),
            pl.when(pl.col("cost_of_service") > 0)
            .then(pl.col("cross_subsidy") / pl.col("cost_of_service"))
            .otherwise(None)
            .alias("pct_overpayment_vs_cos"),
            pl.when(pl.lit(total_kwh) > 0)
            .then(pl.col("kwh_total") / pl.lit(total_kwh))
            .otherwise(None)
            .alias("pct_all_kwh"),
        )
    )

    _sub_rows = cos_tbl.filter(pl.col("subclass") != "All customers")
    _w_sub = _sub_rows["n_customers"].to_numpy()
    _raw = _w_sub * display_customer_total / total_w if total_w > 0 else _w_sub
    _floors = np.floor(_raw).astype(np.int64)
    _rem = display_customer_total - int(_floors.sum())
    _order = np.argsort(-(_raw - _floors))
    for _k in range(max(0, _rem)):
        _floors[_order[_k]] += 1
    _lut_sub = pl.DataFrame(
        {"subclass": _sub_rows["subclass"], "n_customers_display": _floors}
    )
    _lut_total = pl.DataFrame(
        {
            "subclass": ["All customers"],
            "n_customers_display": np.array([display_customer_total], dtype=np.int64),
        }
    )
    return cos_tbl.join(pl.concat([_lut_sub, _lut_total]), on="subclass", how="left").with_columns(
        (pl.col("n_customers_display").cast(pl.Float64) / float(display_customer_total)).alias(
            "pct_customers_display"
        ),
    )


def build_gt_cos_totals(
    cos_tbl: pl.DataFrame,
    *,
    title: str = "Delivery revenue and cost of service by residential customer subclass",
    source_note: str | None = None,
    statewide_narrow: bool = False,
    side_label: str = "Delivery",
) -> GT:
    """If ``statewide_narrow`` is True (statewide-only presentation), omit the Customers column."""
    cos_tbl = _snap_cos_tbl_cross_subsidy_display(cos_tbl)
    _all_data_cols = [
        "n_customers_display",
        "pct_customers_display",
        "pct_all_kwh",
        "revenue_delivery",
        "pct_all_revenue",
        "cost_of_service",
        "pct_revenue_requirement",
        "cross_subsidy",
        "pct_overpayment_vs_cos",
    ]
    _omit_if_narrow = frozenset({"n_customers_display"})
    _data_cols = [c for c in _all_data_cols if not statewide_narrow or c not in _omit_if_narrow]
    _select_cols = ["subclass", *_data_cols]
    _gt_df = cos_tbl.select(_select_cols)
    _label_map = {
        "n_customers_display": "Customers",
        "pct_customers_display": "% of customers",
        "pct_all_kwh": "% of consumption",
        "revenue_delivery": f"{side_label} revenue",
        "pct_all_revenue": "% of revenue",
        "cost_of_service": "Cost of service",
        "pct_revenue_requirement": "% of cost",
        "cross_subsidy": "Cross-subsidy",
        "pct_overpayment_vs_cos": "Cross-subsidy ÷ COS",
    }
    _gt = (
        GT(_gt_df, rowname_col="subclass")
        .tab_header(title=title)
        .tab_stubhead(label="Heating source")
        .tab_options(**_COS_GT_TAB_OPTS)
        .cols_label(**{c: _label_map[c] for c in _data_cols})
    )
    if not statewide_narrow:
        _gt = _gt.fmt_number(columns=["n_customers_display"], decimals=0, use_seps=True)
    _gt = (
        _gt.fmt_percent(
            columns=[
                c
                for c in (
                    "pct_customers_display",
                    "pct_all_kwh",
                    "pct_all_revenue",
                    "pct_revenue_requirement",
                    "pct_overpayment_vs_cos",
                )
                if c in _data_cols
            ],
            decimals=1,
        )
        .fmt_currency(
            columns=["revenue_delivery", "cost_of_service", "cross_subsidy"],
            decimals=0,
            pattern=SB_GT_PER_YEAR_HTML_PATTERN,
        )
        .sub_missing(missing_text="—")
    )
    if source_note is not None:
        _gt = _gt.tab_source_note(source_note)
    return _cos_gt_center_cells(_gt, data_columns=_data_cols)


def build_gt_cos_totals_abridged(
    cos_tbl: pl.DataFrame,
    *,
    title: str = "Delivery revenue by subclass",
    side_label: str = "Delivery",
) -> GT:
    """Statewide summary: customers, consumption (GWh), and delivery revenue (no COS / cross-subsidy)."""
    _cols = [
        "subclass",
        "n_customers_display",
        "pct_customers_display",
        "kwh_total",
        "pct_all_kwh",
        "revenue_delivery",
        "pct_all_revenue",
    ]
    _gt_df = cos_tbl.select(_cols)
    _data_cols = [
        "n_customers_display",
        "pct_customers_display",
        "kwh_total",
        "pct_all_kwh",
        "revenue_delivery",
        "pct_all_revenue",
    ]
    _gt = (
        GT(_gt_df, rowname_col="subclass")
        .tab_header(title=title)
        .tab_stubhead(label="Heating source")
        .tab_options(**_COS_GT_TAB_OPTS)
        .cols_label(
            n_customers_display="Customers",
            pct_customers_display="% of customers",
            kwh_total="Consumption",
            pct_all_kwh="% of consumption",
            revenue_delivery=f"{side_label} revenue",
            pct_all_revenue="% of revenue",
        )
        .fmt_number(columns=["n_customers_display"], decimals=0, use_seps=True)
        .fmt(_fmt_total_kwh_as_gwh, columns=["kwh_total"])
        .fmt_percent(
            columns=["pct_customers_display", "pct_all_kwh", "pct_all_revenue"],
            decimals=1,
        )
        .fmt_currency(
            columns=["revenue_delivery"],
            decimals=0,
            pattern=SB_GT_PER_YEAR_HTML_PATTERN,
        )
        .sub_missing(missing_text="—")
    )
    return (
        _gt.cols_align(align="center", columns=_data_cols)
        .tab_style(
            style=style.text(align="center", weight="bold"),
            locations=loc.stub(),
        )
        .tab_style(
            style=style.text(align="center"),
            locations=loc.column_labels(),
        )
    )


def build_gt_mc_diagnostic(
    bat: pl.DataFrame,
    *,
    title: str,
) -> GT:
    """Build a diagnostic table showing MC (economic burden), residual, and COS by subclass.

    Takes the raw bat DataFrame (which has economic_burden_delivery and residual_delivery)
    and aggregates by heating_type_v2.
    """
    total_w = float(bat["weight"].sum())

    _by_ht = bat.group_by("postprocess_group.heating_type_v2").agg(
        pl.col("weight").sum().alias("n_customers"),
        (pl.col("weight") * pl.col("economic_burden_delivery")).sum().alias("total_mc"),
        (pl.col("weight") * pl.col("residual_delivery")).sum().alias("total_residual"),
        (pl.col("weight") * pl.col("cost_of_service_delivery")).sum().alias("total_cos"),
    )
    _spine = pl.DataFrame({"postprocess_group.heating_type_v2": list(HT_V2_ORDER)})
    _agg_cols_mc = [
        "subclass",
        "n_customers",
        "total_mc",
        "total_residual",
        "total_cos",
    ]
    by_ht = (
        _spine.join(_by_ht, on="postprocess_group.heating_type_v2", how="left")
        .with_columns(
            pl.col("n_customers").fill_null(0.0),
            pl.col("total_mc").fill_null(0.0),
            pl.col("total_residual").fill_null(0.0),
            pl.col("total_cos").fill_null(0.0),
        )
        .with_columns(
            pl.col("postprocess_group.heating_type_v2")
            .replace(HT_V2_LABELS)
            .alias("subclass"),
        )
        .drop("postprocess_group.heating_type_v2")
        .select(_agg_cols_mc)
    )
    _total = pl.DataFrame({
        "subclass": ["All customers"],
        "n_customers": [total_w],
        "total_mc": [float(by_ht["total_mc"].sum())],
        "total_residual": [float(by_ht["total_residual"].sum())],
        "total_cos": [float(by_ht["total_cos"].sum())],
    }).select(_agg_cols_mc)
    tbl = (
        pl.concat([by_ht, _total], how="vertical")
        .with_columns(
            pl.when(pl.col("n_customers") > 0)
            .then(pl.col("total_mc") / pl.col("n_customers"))
            .otherwise(None)
            .alias("avg_mc"),
            pl.when(pl.col("n_customers") > 0)
            .then(pl.col("total_residual") / pl.col("n_customers"))
            .otherwise(None)
            .alias("avg_residual"),
            pl.when(pl.col("n_customers") > 0)
            .then(pl.col("total_cos") / pl.col("n_customers"))
            .otherwise(None)
            .alias("avg_cos"),
        )
        .select(
            "subclass",
            "n_customers",
            "total_mc",
            "avg_mc",
            "total_residual",
            "avg_residual",
            "total_cos",
            "avg_cos",
        )
    )
    _data_cols = [
        "n_customers",
        "total_mc",
        "avg_mc",
        "total_residual",
        "avg_residual",
        "total_cos",
        "avg_cos",
    ]
    _gt = (
        GT(tbl, rowname_col="subclass")
        .tab_header(title=title)
        .tab_stubhead(label="Heating source")
        .tab_options(**_COS_GT_TAB_OPTS)
        .tab_spanner(label="Marginal cost (economic burden)", columns=["total_mc", "avg_mc"])
        .tab_spanner(label="Residual allocation", columns=["total_residual", "avg_residual"])
        .tab_spanner(label="Cost of service (MC + residual)", columns=["total_cos", "avg_cos"])
        .cols_label(
            n_customers="Customers",
            total_mc="Total",
            avg_mc="Avg. per customer",
            total_residual="Total",
            avg_residual="Avg. per customer",
            total_cos="Total",
            avg_cos="Avg. per customer",
        )
        .fmt_number(columns=["n_customers"], decimals=0, use_seps=True)
        .fmt_currency(
            columns=[
                "total_mc",
                "avg_mc",
                "total_residual",
                "avg_residual",
                "total_cos",
                "avg_cos",
            ],
            decimals=0,
        )
        .sub_missing(missing_text="—")
    )
    return _cos_gt_center_cells(_gt, data_columns=_data_cols)


def build_gt_cos_comparison_totals(
    cos_default: pl.DataFrame,
    cos_proposed: pl.DataFrame,
    *,
    title: str,
    label_spanner_today: str,
    label_spanner_proposed: str,
    side_label: str = "Delivery",
) -> GT:
    """Default vs proposed: COS first (no spanner), then two spanner groups (no kWh)."""
    _rev_label = f"{side_label} revenue"
    _delta_label = (
        "Change ($)" if side_label == "Delivery" else f"Δ {side_label.lower()} ($)"
    )
    _cur = cos_default.select(
        "subclass",
        pl.col("revenue_delivery").alias("rev_delivery_current"),
        pl.col("pct_all_revenue").alias("pct_rev_delivery_current"),
        pl.col("cross_subsidy").alias("xsub_default"),
        pl.col("pct_overpayment_vs_cos").alias("pct_xsub_cos_default"),
    )
    _hp = cos_proposed.select(
        "subclass",
        pl.col("revenue_delivery").alias("rev_delivery_proposed"),
        pl.col("pct_all_revenue").alias("pct_rev_delivery_proposed"),
        "cost_of_service",
        "pct_revenue_requirement",
        pl.col("cross_subsidy").alias("xsub_proposed"),
        pl.col("pct_overpayment_vs_cos").alias("pct_xsub_cos_proposed"),
    )
    _gt_df = (
        _cur.join(_hp, on="subclass", how="inner")
        .with_columns(
            (pl.col("rev_delivery_proposed") - pl.col("rev_delivery_current")).alias(
                "delta_rev_delivery"
            ),
        )
        .select(
            "subclass",
            "cost_of_service",
            "rev_delivery_current",
            "pct_rev_delivery_current",
            "xsub_default",
            "pct_xsub_cos_default",
            "delta_rev_delivery",
            "rev_delivery_proposed",
            "pct_rev_delivery_proposed",
            "pct_revenue_requirement",
            "xsub_proposed",
            "pct_xsub_cos_proposed",
        )
    )
    _gt_df = _snap_comparison_totals_cross_subsidy_display(_gt_df)
    _data_cols = [
        "cost_of_service",
        "rev_delivery_current",
        "pct_rev_delivery_current",
        "xsub_default",
        "pct_xsub_cos_default",
        "delta_rev_delivery",
        "rev_delivery_proposed",
        "pct_rev_delivery_proposed",
        "pct_revenue_requirement",
        "xsub_proposed",
        "pct_xsub_cos_proposed",
    ]
    _cmp_table_id = f"gt-cos-cmp-tot-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}"
    _gt = (
        GT(_gt_df, rowname_col="subclass")
        .tab_header(title=title)
        .tab_stubhead(label="Heating source")
        .tab_spanner(
            label=label_spanner_today,
            columns=[
                "rev_delivery_current",
                "pct_rev_delivery_current",
                "xsub_default",
                "pct_xsub_cos_default",
            ],
        )
        .tab_spanner(
            label=label_spanner_proposed,
            columns=[
                "delta_rev_delivery",
                "rev_delivery_proposed",
                "pct_rev_delivery_proposed",
                "pct_revenue_requirement",
                "xsub_proposed",
                "pct_xsub_cos_proposed",
            ],
        )
        .cols_label(
            cost_of_service="Cost of service",
            rev_delivery_current=_rev_label,
            pct_rev_delivery_current="% of total",
            xsub_default="Cross-subsidy",
            pct_xsub_cos_default="Cross-subsidy ÷ COS",
            delta_rev_delivery=_delta_label,
            rev_delivery_proposed=_rev_label,
            pct_rev_delivery_proposed="% of total",
            pct_revenue_requirement="% of cost",
            xsub_proposed="Cross-subsidy",
            pct_xsub_cos_proposed="Cross-subsidy ÷ COS",
        )
        .tab_options(
            **_cos_gt_tab_options_comparison(
                table_width="1480px",
                table_id=_cmp_table_id,
                tbody_nth_after_first_spanner=6,
                thead_leaf_nth_end_first_spanner=4,
            )
        )
        .with_id(_cmp_table_id)
        .cols_width(
            subclass="132px",
            cost_of_service="124px",
            rev_delivery_current="132px",
            pct_rev_delivery_current="86px",
            xsub_default="112px",
            pct_xsub_cos_default="100px",
            delta_rev_delivery="108px",
            rev_delivery_proposed="132px",
            pct_rev_delivery_proposed="86px",
            pct_revenue_requirement="102px",
            xsub_proposed="112px",
            pct_xsub_cos_proposed="100px",
        )
        .fmt_percent(
            columns=[
                "pct_rev_delivery_current",
                "pct_rev_delivery_proposed",
                "pct_revenue_requirement",
                "pct_xsub_cos_default",
                "pct_xsub_cos_proposed",
            ],
            decimals=1,
        )
        .fmt_currency(
            columns=[
                "cost_of_service",
                "rev_delivery_current",
                "delta_rev_delivery",
                "rev_delivery_proposed",
                "xsub_default",
                "xsub_proposed",
            ],
            decimals=0,
            pattern=SB_GT_PER_YEAR_HTML_PATTERN,
        )
        .sub_missing(missing_text="—")
    )
    return _cos_gt_center_cells(_gt, data_columns=_data_cols)


def build_gt_cos_avg(
    cos_tbl: pl.DataFrame,
    *,
    title: str = "Average delivery bill, COS, and cross-subsidy by subclass (per customer-year)",
    source_note: str | None = None,
    side_label: str = "Delivery",
) -> GT:
    _avg_tbl = (
        cos_tbl.select(
            "subclass",
            "n_customers",
            "pct_customers_display",
            "pct_all_kwh",
            "kwh_total",
            "revenue_delivery",
            "cost_of_service",
            "cross_subsidy",
        )
        .with_columns(
            pl.when(pl.col("n_customers") > 0)
            .then(pl.col("kwh_total") / pl.col("n_customers"))
            .otherwise(None)
            .alias("avg_kwh"),
            pl.when(pl.col("n_customers") > 0)
            .then(pl.col("revenue_delivery") / pl.col("n_customers"))
            .otherwise(None)
            .alias("avg_delivery_bill"),
            pl.when(pl.col("n_customers") > 0)
            .then(pl.col("cost_of_service") / pl.col("n_customers"))
            .otherwise(None)
            .alias("avg_cost_of_service"),
            pl.when(pl.col("n_customers") > 0)
            .then(pl.col("cross_subsidy") / pl.col("n_customers"))
            .otherwise(None)
            .alias("avg_cross_subsidy"),
        )
        .with_columns(
            pl.when(pl.col("avg_cross_subsidy").abs() < 0.5)
            .then(0.0)
            .otherwise(pl.col("avg_cross_subsidy"))
            .alias("avg_cross_subsidy"),
        )
        .with_columns(
            pl.when(
                (pl.col("avg_cost_of_service").is_not_null())
                & (pl.col("avg_cost_of_service") > 0)
            )
            .then(pl.col("avg_cross_subsidy") / pl.col("avg_cost_of_service"))
            .otherwise(None)
            .alias("avg_bat_div_avg_cos"),
        )
        .select(
            "subclass",
            "pct_customers_display",
            "pct_all_kwh",
            "avg_kwh",
            "avg_delivery_bill",
            "avg_cost_of_service",
            "avg_cross_subsidy",
            "avg_bat_div_avg_cos",
        )
    )
    _gt = (
        GT(_avg_tbl, rowname_col="subclass")
        .tab_header(title=title)
        .tab_stubhead(label="Heating source")
        .tab_options(**_COS_GT_TAB_OPTS)
        .cols_label(
            pct_customers_display="% of customers",
            pct_all_kwh="% of consumption",
            avg_kwh="Avg. consumption",
            avg_delivery_bill=f"Avg. {side_label.lower()} bill",
            avg_cost_of_service="Avg. cost of service",
            avg_cross_subsidy="Avg. cross-subsidy",
            avg_bat_div_avg_cos="Avg. cross-subsidy ÷ Avg. COS",
        )
        .fmt_percent(
            columns=["pct_customers_display", "pct_all_kwh", "avg_bat_div_avg_cos"],
            decimals=1,
        )
        .fmt(_fmt_avg_kwh_per_customer, columns=["avg_kwh"])
        .fmt_currency(
            columns=["avg_delivery_bill", "avg_cost_of_service", "avg_cross_subsidy"],
            decimals=0,
            pattern=SB_GT_PER_YEAR_HTML_PATTERN,
        )
        .sub_missing(missing_text="—")
    )
    if source_note is not None:
        _gt = _gt.tab_source_note(source_note)
    return _cos_gt_center_cells(
        _gt,
        data_columns=[
            "pct_customers_display",
            "pct_all_kwh",
            "avg_kwh",
            "avg_delivery_bill",
            "avg_cost_of_service",
            "avg_cross_subsidy",
            "avg_bat_div_avg_cos",
        ],
    )


def build_gt_cos_comparison_avg(
    cos_default: pl.DataFrame,
    cos_proposed: pl.DataFrame,
    *,
    title: str,
    label_spanner_default: str,
    label_spanner_proposed: str,
    side_label: str = "Delivery",
) -> GT:
    """Per-customer averages: avg. COS first (no spanner), then two spanner groups (no kWh)."""
    _bill_l = f"Avg. {side_label.lower()} bill"
    _delta_bill_l = f"Change in avg. {side_label.lower()} bill"

    def _avg_side(df: pl.DataFrame) -> pl.DataFrame:
        return (
            df.select(
                "subclass",
                "n_customers",
                "revenue_delivery",
                "cost_of_service",
                "cross_subsidy",
            )
            .with_columns(
                pl.when(pl.col("n_customers") > 0)
                .then(pl.col("revenue_delivery") / pl.col("n_customers"))
                .otherwise(None)
                .alias("avg_delivery_bill"),
                pl.when(pl.col("n_customers") > 0)
                .then(pl.col("cost_of_service") / pl.col("n_customers"))
                .otherwise(None)
                .alias("avg_cost_of_service"),
                pl.when(pl.col("n_customers") > 0)
                .then(pl.col("cross_subsidy") / pl.col("n_customers"))
                .otherwise(None)
                .alias("avg_cross_subsidy"),
            )
            .with_columns(
                pl.when(pl.col("avg_cross_subsidy").abs() < 0.5)
                .then(0.0)
                .otherwise(pl.col("avg_cross_subsidy"))
                .alias("avg_cross_subsidy"),
            )
            .with_columns(
                pl.when(
                    (pl.col("avg_cost_of_service").is_not_null())
                    & (pl.col("avg_cost_of_service") > 0)
                )
                .then(pl.col("avg_cross_subsidy") / pl.col("avg_cost_of_service"))
                .otherwise(None)
                .alias("avg_bat_div_avg_cos"),
            )
        )

    _c = _avg_side(cos_default).select(
        "subclass",
        pl.col("avg_delivery_bill").alias("avg_del_default"),
        pl.col("avg_cross_subsidy").alias("avg_xsub_default"),
    )
    _h = _avg_side(cos_proposed).select(
        "subclass",
        pl.col("avg_delivery_bill").alias("avg_del_proposed"),
        pl.col("avg_cost_of_service").alias("avg_cos_proposed"),
        pl.col("avg_cross_subsidy").alias("avg_xsub_proposed"),
        pl.col("avg_bat_div_avg_cos").alias("avg_ratio_proposed"),
    )
    _gt_df = (
        _c.join(_h, on="subclass", how="inner")
        .with_columns(
            (pl.col("avg_del_proposed") - pl.col("avg_del_default")).alias(
                "delta_avg_delivery"
            ),
        )
        .select(
            "subclass",
            "avg_cos_proposed",
            "avg_del_default",
            "avg_xsub_default",
            "delta_avg_delivery",
            "avg_del_proposed",
            "avg_xsub_proposed",
            "avg_ratio_proposed",
        )
    )
    _gt_df = _gt_df.with_columns(
        pl.when(pl.col("delta_avg_delivery").abs() < 0.5)
        .then(0.0)
        .otherwise(pl.col("delta_avg_delivery"))
        .alias("delta_avg_delivery"),
    )
    _cmp_table_id = f"gt-cos-cmp-avg-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}"
    _gt = (
        GT(_gt_df, rowname_col="subclass")
        .tab_header(title=title)
        .tab_stubhead(label="Heating source")
        .tab_spanner(
            label=label_spanner_default,
            columns=["avg_del_default", "avg_xsub_default"],
        )
        .tab_spanner(
            label=label_spanner_proposed,
            columns=[
                "delta_avg_delivery",
                "avg_del_proposed",
                "avg_xsub_proposed",
                "avg_ratio_proposed",
            ],
        )
        .cols_label(
            avg_cos_proposed="Avg. cost of service",
            avg_del_default=_bill_l,
            avg_xsub_default="Avg. cross-subsidy",
            delta_avg_delivery=_delta_bill_l,
            avg_del_proposed=_bill_l,
            avg_xsub_proposed="Avg. cross-subsidy",
            avg_ratio_proposed="Avg. cross-subsidy ÷ Avg. COS",
        )
        .tab_options(
            **_cos_gt_tab_options_comparison(
                table_width="1280px",
                table_id=_cmp_table_id,
                tbody_nth_after_first_spanner=4,
                thead_leaf_nth_end_first_spanner=2,
            )
        )
        .with_id(_cmp_table_id)
        .cols_width(
            subclass="132px",
            avg_cos_proposed="124px",
            avg_del_default="128px",
            avg_xsub_default="128px",
            delta_avg_delivery="148px",
            avg_del_proposed="128px",
            avg_xsub_proposed="118px",
            avg_ratio_proposed="112px",
        )
        .fmt_percent(columns=["avg_ratio_proposed"], decimals=1)
        .fmt_currency(
            columns=[
                "avg_cos_proposed",
                "avg_del_default",
                "avg_xsub_default",
                "delta_avg_delivery",
                "avg_del_proposed",
                "avg_xsub_proposed",
            ],
            decimals=0,
            pattern=SB_GT_PER_YEAR_HTML_PATTERN,
        )
        .sub_missing(missing_text="—")
    )
    return _cos_gt_center_cells(
        _gt,
        data_columns=[
            "avg_cos_proposed",
            "avg_del_default",
            "avg_xsub_default",
            "delta_avg_delivery",
            "avg_del_proposed",
            "avg_xsub_proposed",
            "avg_ratio_proposed",
        ],
    )


def load_scenario_proposed_cos(
    scenario: dict,
) -> tuple[pl.DataFrame, pl.DataFrame, dict[str, pl.DataFrame]]:
    """Load proposed-run master BAT and build statewide + per-utility COS tables."""
    run = scenario["run"]
    alloc = scenario["residual_alloc"]
    label = scenario["label"]
    path_proposed = (
        f"{S3_BASE}/{STATE_LOWER}/all_utilities/{BATCH}/run_{run}"
        "/cross_subsidization_BAT_values/"
    )
    bat_proposed = load_master_bat(path_proposed, residual_alloc=alloc)
    bat_proposed = join_resstock_annual_elec_kwh(
        bat_proposed,
        path_state_monthly=PATH_RESSTOCK_NY_LOAD_CURVE_MONTHLY,
        run_pair=run,
        path_cache_dir=PATH_COS_NOTEBOOK_CACHE,
    )
    assert_bill_identity(bat_proposed, f"run {run} ({label})")
    cos_proposed_sw = build_cos_table(bat_proposed, rev_req=statewide_delivery_rr)
    cos_proposed_by_util: dict[str, pl.DataFrame] = {}
    for util in UTILITIES:
        cos_proposed_by_util[util] = build_cos_table(
            bat_proposed,
            rev_req=rev_req[util]["total_delivery_rr"],
            utility_filter=util,
        )
    return bat_proposed, cos_proposed_sw, cos_proposed_by_util


def load_scenario_proposed_cos_supply(
    scenario: dict,
) -> tuple[pl.DataFrame, pl.DataFrame, dict[str, pl.DataFrame]]:
    """Load proposed-run master BAT (supply side) and build statewide + per-utility COS tables."""
    run = scenario["run"]
    alloc = scenario["residual_alloc"]
    label = scenario["label"]
    path_proposed = (
        f"{S3_BASE}/{STATE_LOWER}/all_utilities/{BATCH}/run_{run}"
        "/cross_subsidization_BAT_values/"
    )
    bat_proposed = load_master_bat_supply(path_proposed, residual_alloc=alloc)
    bat_proposed = join_resstock_annual_elec_kwh(
        bat_proposed,
        path_state_monthly=PATH_RESSTOCK_NY_LOAD_CURVE_MONTHLY,
        run_pair=run,
        path_cache_dir=PATH_COS_NOTEBOOK_CACHE,
    )
    assert_bill_identity(bat_proposed, f"run {run} ({label}, supply)")
    cos_proposed_sw = build_cos_table(bat_proposed, rev_req=statewide_supply_rr)
    cos_proposed_by_util: dict[str, pl.DataFrame] = {}
    for util in UTILITIES:
        cos_proposed_by_util[util] = build_cos_table(
            bat_proposed,
            rev_req=rev_req[util]["total_supply_rr"],
            utility_filter=util,
        )
    return bat_proposed, cos_proposed_sw, cos_proposed_by_util


def fill_report_vars_for_scenario(
    sid: str,
    cos_proposed_sw: pl.DataFrame,
    cos_proposed_by_util: dict[str, pl.DataFrame],
) -> None:
    """Populate ``report_vars[sid]`` with statewide and per-utility HP stats."""
    report_vars[sid] = {"statewide": {}, "by_utility": {}}

    _hp_row_sw = cos_proposed_sw.filter(pl.col("subclass") == "Heat pump")
    _all_row_sw = cos_proposed_sw.filter(pl.col("subclass") == "All customers")
    report_vars[sid]["statewide"] = {
        "total_customers": float(_all_row_sw["n_customers"][0]),
        "total_delivery_rev": float(_all_row_sw["revenue_delivery"][0]),
        "hp_cross_subsidy": float(_hp_row_sw["cross_subsidy"][0]),
        "hp_pct_overpayment": float(_hp_row_sw["pct_overpayment_vs_cos"][0])
        if _hp_row_sw["pct_overpayment_vs_cos"][0] is not None
        else None,
    }

    for util in UTILITIES:
        _cos_p = cos_proposed_by_util[util]
        _hp_u = _cos_p.filter(pl.col("subclass") == "Heat pump")
        report_vars[sid]["by_utility"][util] = {
            "hp_cross_subsidy": float(_hp_u["cross_subsidy"][0]),
            "hp_pct_overpayment": float(_hp_u["pct_overpayment_vs_cos"][0])
            if _hp_u["pct_overpayment_vs_cos"][0] is not None
            else None,
        }


def calibrated_volumetric_c_per_kwh(
    utility: str, tariff_fragment: str
) -> str:
    """Extract first energy tier from a calibrated tariff JSON → ¢/kWh string."""
    path = f"{_RDP_TARIFF_DIR}/{utility}_{tariff_fragment}_calibrated.json"
    doc = parse_urdb_json(fetch_rdp_file(path, RDP_REF))
    rate = float(doc["items"][0]["energyratestructure"][0][0]["rate"])
    return f"{rate * 100:.2f}¢/kWh"
```

## Load revenue requirements

We fetch each utility's base YAML and HP-vs-non-HP YAML from the rate-design-platform repo. The base YAML provides `total_delivery_revenue_requirement` and `total_residential_kwh`. The subclass YAML uses the **nested format**: `subclass_revenue_requirements.delivery.{alloc}` gives HP vs non-HP delivery splits for each allocation method, and `heating_type_breakdown.{alloc}` gives per-heating-type COS (for validation against BAT-derived values).

```{python}
#| label: load-rev-req

_RDP_RR_DIR = f"rate_design/hp_rates/{STATE_LOWER}/config/rev_requirement"

rev_req: dict[str, dict] = {}

for util in UTILITIES:
    _base_raw = fetch_rdp_file(f"{_RDP_RR_DIR}/{util}.yaml", RDP_REF)
    _base = yaml.safe_load(_base_raw)

    _hp_raw = fetch_rdp_file(f"{_RDP_RR_DIR}/{util}_hp_vs_nonhp.yaml", RDP_REF)
    _hp = yaml.safe_load(_hp_raw)

    _sub_rr = _hp["subclass_revenue_requirements"]
    _ht_bk = _hp["heating_type_breakdown"]

    rev_req[util] = {
        "total_delivery_rr": float(_base["total_delivery_revenue_requirement"]),
        "total_supply_rr": float(_base["supply_revenue_requirement_topups"]),
        "total_residential_kwh": float(_base["total_residential_kwh"]),
        "subclass_rr": _sub_rr,
        "heating_type_breakdown": _ht_bk,
    }

statewide_delivery_rr = sum(v["total_delivery_rr"] for v in rev_req.values())
statewide_supply_rr = sum(v["total_supply_rr"] for v in rev_req.values())
statewide_residential_kwh = sum(v["total_residential_kwh"] for v in rev_req.values())

print(f"Statewide delivery RR: ${statewide_delivery_rr:,.0f}")
print(f"Statewide supply RR: ${statewide_supply_rr:,.0f}")
print(f"Statewide residential kWh: {statewide_residential_kwh:,.0f}")
for util in UTILITIES:
    rr = rev_req[util]
    _sub = rr["subclass_rr"]["delivery"]
    _alloc_methods = [k for k in _sub if k != "passthrough"]
    print(
        f"  {UTIL_DISPLAY[util]:20s}  "
        f"delivery RR ${rr['total_delivery_rr']:>16,.0f}   "
        f"supply RR ${rr['total_supply_rr']:>16,.0f}   "
        f"alloc methods: {_alloc_methods}"
    )
    for alloc in _alloc_methods:
        _hp_d = float(_sub[alloc]["hp"])
        _nonhp_d = float(_sub[alloc]["non-hp"])
        print(f"    {alloc:14s}  HP ${_hp_d:>14,.0f}   non-HP ${_nonhp_d:>16,.0f}")
```

---

# PER-CUSTOMER SUPPLY RESIDUAL {#sec-percustomer-supply}

The tables below mirror the delivery-side COS tables, but use **only the supply** portion of each customer's electric bill: `annual_bill_supply`, economic burden, residual share, and `BAT_percustomer_supply`. Revenue requirement denominators use each utility's **supply revenue requirement** (`supply_revenue_requirement_topups` in the rev-requirement YAMLs). The baseline is run 1+2; the proposed comparison is HP seasonal (run 5+6), with per-customer residual on supply (the only supply residual method in this batch).

## Load baseline data (supply, per-customer residual)

```{python}
#| label: load-default-supply-pc

bat_default_supply_pc = load_master_bat_supply(
    PATH_MASTER_BAT_DEFAULT, residual_alloc="percustomer"
)
bat_default_supply_pc = join_resstock_annual_elec_kwh(
    bat_default_supply_pc,
    path_state_monthly=PATH_RESSTOCK_NY_LOAD_CURVE_MONTHLY,
    run_pair=RUN_DEFAULT,
    path_cache_dir=PATH_COS_NOTEBOOK_CACHE,
)
assert_bill_identity(bat_default_supply_pc, "run 1+2 (default, percustomer, supply)")

cos_default_sw_supply_pc = build_cos_table(
    bat_default_supply_pc, rev_req=statewide_supply_rr
)

cos_default_by_util_supply_pc: dict[str, pl.DataFrame] = {}
for util in UTILITIES:
    cos_default_by_util_supply_pc[util] = build_cos_table(
        bat_default_supply_pc,
        rev_req=rev_req[util]["total_supply_rr"],
        utility_filter=util,
    )

print(f"Default BAT supply (percustomer) loaded: {bat_default_supply_pc.height:,} buildings")
print(f"  Weighted supply revenue: ${(bat_default_supply_pc['weight'] * bat_default_supply_pc['annual_bill_delivery']).sum():,.0f}")
```

## Baseline statewide — totals (supply, per-customer residual)

```{python}
#| label: tbl-baseline-sw-totals-supply-pc
#| tbl-cap: "Total supply revenue, supply cost of service, and cross-subsidy by residential customer subclass, statewide, under each utility's default rate. Per-customer supply residual only."

display_gt(
    build_gt_cos_totals(
        cos_default_sw_supply_pc,
        title=cos_baseline_totals_title(None, cos_default_sw_supply_pc),
        source_note=cos_table_baseline_source_note(),
        statewide_narrow=True,
        side_label="Supply",
    )
)
```

## Baseline statewide — averages (supply, per-customer residual)

```{python}
#| label: tbl-baseline-sw-avg-supply-pc
#| tbl-cap: "Average annual electric supply bill, supply cost of service, and cross-subsidy by residential customer subclass, under default rates. Per-customer supply residual only."

display_gt(
    build_gt_cos_avg(
        cos_default_sw_supply_pc,
        title=cos_baseline_avg_title(None, cos_default_sw_supply_pc),
        source_note=cos_table_baseline_source_note(),
        side_label="Supply",
    )
)
```

## Baseline statewide — marginal cost diagnostic (supply, per-customer residual)

This table decomposes **supply** cost of service into **marginal cost** (economic burden) and **residual allocation**. The MC column should be identical regardless of allocation method — only the residual changes.

```{python}
#| label: tbl-mc-diagnostic-sw-supply-pc
#| tbl-cap: "Supply marginal cost, residual, and COS decomposition by subclass — NY statewide (per-customer supply residual)"

display_gt(
    build_gt_mc_diagnostic(
        bat_default_supply_pc,
        title="Marginal cost, residual, and COS decomposition — NY statewide (supply)",
    )
)
```

## HP seasonal vs. baseline statewide — totals (supply, per-customer residual)

```{python}
#| label: load-hp-seasonal-supply-pc

bat_hp_seasonal_supply_pc, cos_hp_seasonal_sw_supply_pc, cos_hp_seasonal_by_util_supply_pc = (
    load_scenario_proposed_cos_supply(SCENARIOS[0])
)
print(
    f"HP seasonal supply (percustomer) loaded: {bat_hp_seasonal_supply_pc.height:,} buildings"
)
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-hp-seasonal-sw-totals-supply-pc
#| tbl-cap: "Default vs HP seasonal supply revenue, COS, and cross-subsidy — NY statewide (per-customer supply residual)"

_sc = SCENARIOS[0]
_spanner_today_supply = "All customers on utility's default rates"
_spanner_proposed_supply = (
    f"HP customers on {_sc['hp_tariff'].replace('_', ' ')} rate; "
    f"non-HP customers on adjusted default rate"
)
display_gt(
    build_gt_cos_comparison_totals(
        cos_default_sw_supply_pc,
        cos_hp_seasonal_sw_supply_pc,
        title="Heat pump rate eliminates cross-subsidy from electric customers with heat pumps to those who heat with fossil fuels",
        label_spanner_today=_spanner_today_supply,
        label_spanner_proposed=_spanner_proposed_supply,
        side_label="Supply",
    )
)
```

## HP seasonal vs. baseline statewide — averages (supply, per-customer residual)

```{python}
#| label: tbl-hp-seasonal-sw-avg-supply-pc
#| tbl-cap: "Average supply bill, supply cost of service, and cross-subsidy by subclass under default vs HP seasonal proposed rates. Per-customer supply residual only."

display_gt(
    build_gt_cos_comparison_avg(
        cos_default_sw_supply_pc,
        cos_hp_seasonal_sw_supply_pc,
        title="Heat pump rate eliminates cross-subsidy from electric customers with heat pumps to those who heat with fossil fuels",
        label_spanner_default=_spanner_today_supply,
        label_spanner_proposed=_spanner_proposed_supply,
        side_label="Supply",
    )
)
```

## Baseline per-utility (supply, per-customer residual)

```{python}
#| label: baseline-per-util-supply-pc

for util in UTILITIES:
    _label_u = UTIL_DISPLAY[util]
    _cos_u = cos_default_by_util_supply_pc[util]
    display_gt(
        build_gt_cos_totals(
            _cos_u,
            title=cos_baseline_totals_title(_label_u, _cos_u),
            source_note=cos_table_baseline_source_note(),
            side_label="Supply",
        )
    )
    display_gt(
        build_gt_cos_avg(
            _cos_u,
            title=cos_baseline_avg_title(_label_u, _cos_u),
            source_note=cos_table_baseline_source_note(),
            side_label="Supply",
        )
    )
print("Displayed baseline per-utility tables (supply, percustomer)")
```

---

# VOLUMETRIC SUPPLY RESIDUAL {#sec-vol-supply}

These tables show the **supply-side** cross-subsidy under **volumetric residual allocation** — where each customer's supply residual is proportional to their annual kWh, rather than split equally across all customers.

We compute this two ways and compare them as a diagnostic:

- **Method A** reads `BAT_vol_supply` from the master BAT parquet (computed by CAIRO as `BAT_vol_total − BAT_vol_delivery`), then derives the residual from the billing identity.
- **Method B** takes the per-customer supply data already loaded above, computes each utility's total residual pool, and redistributes it proportionally to each customer's **net** kWh. This never touches `BAT_vol_supply`.

**Expected divergence:** CAIRO's `electricity_net` (used for its volumetric BAT) is **not** the same as ResStock's `out.electricity.net.energy_consumption`. CAIRO computes `electricity_net` from `out.electricity.total.energy_consumption` + custom PV sign-correction logic in `patches.py`; ResStock's built-in net column uses different sign conventions and diverges even for non-solar buildings (see `utils/loads.py` warning). Since we can't replicate CAIRO's exact PV logic from monthly data alone, Method B is an approximation. The total residual pool per utility matches exactly — only the per-customer kWh shares differ due to the `electricity_net` definition mismatch.

## Load volumetric supply data and compare methods

```{python}
#| label: load-vol-supply-and-compare

# --- Method A: from BAT_vol_supply column ---
bat_vol_supply_a = load_master_bat_supply(
    PATH_MASTER_BAT_DEFAULT, residual_alloc="vol"
)
bat_vol_supply_a = join_resstock_annual_elec_kwh(
    bat_vol_supply_a,
    path_state_monthly=PATH_RESSTOCK_NY_LOAD_CURVE_MONTHLY,
    run_pair=RUN_DEFAULT,
    path_cache_dir=PATH_COS_NOTEBOOK_CACHE,
)
assert_bill_identity(bat_vol_supply_a, "run 1+2 (default, vol supply — Method A)")

# --- Method B: independent estimate from per-customer data, using net kWh ---
bat_default_supply_pc_with_net = join_resstock_annual_elec_net_kwh(
    bat_default_supply_pc,
    path_state_monthly=PATH_RESSTOCK_NY_LOAD_CURVE_MONTHLY,
    run_pair=RUN_DEFAULT,
    path_cache_dir=PATH_COS_NOTEBOOK_CACHE,
)
bat_vol_supply_b = estimate_volumetric_supply_bat(
    bat_default_supply_pc_with_net, kwh_col="annual_elec_kwh_net"
)
assert_bill_identity(bat_vol_supply_b, "run 1+2 (default, vol supply — Method B estimate)")

# --- Compare BAT ---
_cmp = bat_vol_supply_a.select("bldg_id", "bat_delivery").rename({"bat_delivery": "bat_a"}).join(
    bat_vol_supply_b.select("bldg_id", "bat_delivery").rename({"bat_delivery": "bat_b"}),
    on="bldg_id",
    how="inner",
)
_cmp = _cmp.with_columns((pl.col("bat_a") - pl.col("bat_b")).abs().alias("abs_diff"))
_max_diff = float(_cmp["abs_diff"].max())  # type: ignore[arg-type]
_mean_diff = float(_cmp["abs_diff"].mean())  # type: ignore[arg-type]
_p99_diff = float(_cmp["abs_diff"].quantile(0.99))  # type: ignore[arg-type]

print("=== Volumetric supply BAT: Method A (parquet) vs Method B (estimate, net kWh) ===")
print(f"  Buildings compared: {_cmp.height:,}")
print(f"  Max |BAT_a − BAT_b|:  ${_max_diff:,.2f}")
print(f"  Mean |BAT_a − BAT_b|: ${_mean_diff:,.2f}")
print(f"  P99 |BAT_a − BAT_b|:  ${_p99_diff:,.2f}")

# --- Compare residual ---
_cmp_res = bat_vol_supply_a.select("bldg_id", "residual_delivery").rename({"residual_delivery": "res_a"}).join(
    bat_vol_supply_b.select("bldg_id", "residual_delivery").rename({"residual_delivery": "res_b"}),
    on="bldg_id",
    how="inner",
)
_cmp_res = _cmp_res.with_columns((pl.col("res_a") - pl.col("res_b")).abs().alias("abs_diff"))
_max_res_diff = float(_cmp_res["abs_diff"].max())  # type: ignore[arg-type]
print(f"  Max |residual_A − residual_B|: ${_max_res_diff:,.2f}")

# Divergence is expected: CAIRO's electricity_net (from patches.py PV sign correction applied
# to out.electricity.total) differs from ResStock's out.electricity.net.energy_consumption.
# The total residual pool per utility matches exactly — only per-customer kWh shares differ.
print("\n  (Divergence expected: CAIRO's electricity_net != ResStock's out.electricity.net;")
print("   total residual pools match exactly per utility — only per-customer kWh shares differ.)")
```

## Build COS tables (volumetric supply)

Using Method A (from `BAT_vol_supply`) for the tables — it is the exact CAIRO computation.

```{python}
#| label: build-cos-vol-supply

cos_default_sw_supply_vol = build_cos_table(
    bat_vol_supply_a, rev_req=statewide_supply_rr
)
print(f"Volumetric supply COS table built: {cos_default_sw_supply_vol.height} subclasses")
```

## Baseline statewide — totals (supply, volumetric residual)

```{python}
#| label: tbl-baseline-sw-totals-supply-vol
#| tbl-cap: "Total supply revenue, supply cost of service, and cross-subsidy by residential customer subclass, under default rates. Volumetric residual allocation."

display_gt(
    build_gt_cos_totals(
        cos_default_sw_supply_vol,
        title=cos_baseline_totals_title(None, cos_default_sw_supply_vol),
        source_note=cos_table_baseline_source_note(),
        statewide_narrow=True,
        side_label="Supply",
    )
)
```

## Baseline statewide — averages (supply, volumetric residual)

```{python}
#| label: tbl-baseline-sw-avg-supply-vol
#| tbl-cap: "Average supply bill, supply cost of service, and cross-subsidy by residential customer subclass, under default rates. Volumetric residual allocation."

display_gt(
    build_gt_cos_avg(
        cos_default_sw_supply_vol,
        title=cos_baseline_avg_title(None, cos_default_sw_supply_vol),
        source_note=cos_table_baseline_source_note(),
        side_label="Supply",
    )
)
```

---

# SUPPLY PASSTHROUGH {#sec-supply-passthrough}

**Supply passthrough** matches the subclass revenue requirement logic in `utils/mid/compute_subclass_rr.py` (rate-design-platform): supply RR is the **actual supply bills** per subclass (no BAT adjustment). The tariff is unchanged from default rates.

At the **customer** level we model this by setting each customer's supply residual to whatever closes the gap between their bill and their marginal cost: `residual = annual_bill_supply − economic_burden_supply`. Then `cost_of_service = bill` and **`BAT = 0` by construction** — so the cross-subsidy column in the totals/averages tables is always zero. The tables still show how current supply charges split between marginal cost and implicit residual; the marginal-cost diagnostic is the main substantive view.

## Build supply passthrough BAT frame and COS table

```{python}
#| label: build-cos-passthrough-supply

bat_passthrough_supply = bat_default_supply_pc.with_columns(
    (pl.col("annual_bill_delivery") - pl.col("economic_burden_delivery")).alias(
        "residual_delivery"
    ),
).with_columns(
    (pl.col("economic_burden_delivery") + pl.col("residual_delivery")).alias(
        "cost_of_service_delivery"
    ),
    pl.lit(0.0).alias("bat_delivery"),
)
assert_bill_identity(bat_passthrough_supply, "run 1+2 (default, supply passthrough)")

cos_default_sw_supply_pt = build_cos_table(
    bat_passthrough_supply, rev_req=statewide_supply_rr
)
print(f"Supply passthrough COS table built: {cos_default_sw_supply_pt.height} subclasses")
```

## Baseline statewide — totals (supply, passthrough)

```{python}
#| label: tbl-baseline-sw-totals-supply-pt
#| tbl-cap: "Total supply revenue, supply cost of service, and cross-subsidy by residential customer subclass, under default rates. Supply passthrough (cross-subsidy zero by construction)."

display_gt(
    build_gt_cos_totals(
        cos_default_sw_supply_pt,
        title=cos_baseline_totals_title(None, cos_default_sw_supply_pt),
        source_note=cos_table_baseline_source_note(),
        statewide_narrow=True,
        side_label="Supply",
    )
)
```

## Baseline statewide — averages (supply, passthrough)

```{python}
#| label: tbl-baseline-sw-avg-supply-pt
#| tbl-cap: "Average supply bill, supply cost of service, and cross-subsidy by residential customer subclass, under default rates. Supply passthrough (cross-subsidy zero by construction)."

display_gt(
    build_gt_cos_avg(
        cos_default_sw_supply_pt,
        title=cos_baseline_avg_title(None, cos_default_sw_supply_pt),
        source_note=cos_table_baseline_source_note(),
        side_label="Supply",
    )
)
```

## Baseline statewide — marginal cost diagnostic (supply, passthrough)

```{python}
#| label: tbl-mc-diagnostic-sw-supply-pt
#| tbl-cap: "Supply marginal cost, residual, and COS decomposition by subclass — NY statewide (supply passthrough)"

display_gt(
    build_gt_mc_diagnostic(
        bat_passthrough_supply,
        title="Marginal cost, residual, and COS decomposition — NY statewide (supply)",
    )
)
```

---

# PER-CUSTOMER DELIVERY RESIDUAL, PER-CUSTOMER SUPPLY RESIDUAL {#sec-percustomer}

All tables in this section use **per-customer residual allocation** for both delivery and supply. The baseline is run 1+2 (default uniform rates); the proposed scenarios are HP seasonal (run 5+6) and HP seasonal TOU flex (run 13+14).

## Load baseline data (per-customer residual)

```{python}
#| label: load-default-pc

bat_default_pc = load_master_bat(
    PATH_MASTER_BAT_DEFAULT, residual_alloc="percustomer"
)
bat_default_pc = join_resstock_annual_elec_kwh(
    bat_default_pc,
    path_state_monthly=PATH_RESSTOCK_NY_LOAD_CURVE_MONTHLY,
    run_pair=RUN_DEFAULT,
    path_cache_dir=PATH_COS_NOTEBOOK_CACHE,
)
assert_bill_identity(bat_default_pc, "run 1+2 (default, percustomer)")

cos_default_sw_pc = build_cos_table(bat_default_pc, rev_req=statewide_delivery_rr)

cos_default_by_util_pc: dict[str, pl.DataFrame] = {}
for util in UTILITIES:
    cos_default_by_util_pc[util] = build_cos_table(
        bat_default_pc, rev_req=rev_req[util]["total_delivery_rr"], utility_filter=util
    )

print(f"Default BAT (percustomer) loaded: {bat_default_pc.height:,} buildings")
print(f"  Weighted customer count: {bat_default_pc['weight'].sum():,.0f}")
print(f"  Weighted delivery revenue: ${(bat_default_pc['weight'] * bat_default_pc['annual_bill_delivery']).sum():,.0f}")
```

## Baseline statewide — summary (per-customer residual)

```{python}
#| label: tbl-baseline-sw-summary-pc
#| tbl-cap: "Total residential customers, electricity consumption, and delivery revenue by heating source, statewide, under each utility's default rate. Total customer counts are from EIA form-861, subclass customer counts are derived from RECS 2020, total revenue is derived from utility rate cases and 2025 delivery rates, subclass consumption comes from ResStock 2024 loads, subclass revenue comes from Switchbox bill calculations, using 2025 utility delivery rates (fixed and volumetric) applied to ResStock loads geographically assigned to each utility."

display_gt(
    build_gt_cos_totals_abridged(
        cos_default_sw_pc,
        title=cos_sw_summary_abridged_title(cos_default_sw_pc),
    )
)
```

## Baseline statewide — totals (per-customer residual)

```{python}
#| label: tbl-baseline-sw-totals-pc
#| tbl-cap: "Total electric delivery revenue, electric delivery cost of service, and cross-subsidy by residential customer heating source subclass, statewide, under each utility's default rate. Assumes lump-sum residual allocation (equal residual share for all customers). Subclass cost-of-service calculated using NREL's CAIRO model, applied to: a. utility-level revenue requirements, b. ResStock 2024 loads (assigned to each utility based on geographic location), c. zonal marginal transmission costs from NYISO, and d. marginal distribution costs from each utility's 2025 MCOS study. Full details available in methods section."

display_gt(
    build_gt_cos_totals(
        cos_default_sw_pc,
        title=cos_baseline_totals_title(None, cos_default_sw_pc),
        source_note=cos_table_baseline_source_note(),
        statewide_narrow=True,
    )
)
```

## Baseline statewide — averages (per-customer residual)

```{python}
#| label: tbl-baseline-sw-avg-pc
#| tbl-cap: "Average annual electric delivery bill, electric delivery cost of service, and cross-subsidy by residential customer subclass, under default rates. Assumes lump-sum residual allocation (equal residual share for all customers)."

display_gt(
    build_gt_cos_avg(
        cos_default_sw_pc,
        title=cos_baseline_avg_title(None, cos_default_sw_pc),
        source_note=cos_table_baseline_source_note(),
    )
)
```

## Baseline statewide — marginal cost diagnostic (per-customer residual)

This table decomposes cost of service into its two components: **marginal cost** (economic burden) and **residual allocation**. The MC column should be identical regardless of allocation method — only the residual changes.

```{python}
#| label: tbl-mc-diagnostic-sw-pc
#| tbl-cap: "Marginal cost, residual, and COS decomposition by subclass — NY statewide (per-customer residual)"

display_gt(
    build_gt_mc_diagnostic(
        bat_default_pc,
        title="Marginal cost, residual, and COS decomposition — NY statewide",
    )
)
```

## HP seasonal vs. baseline statewide — totals (per-customer residual)

```{python}
#| label: load-hp-seasonal-pc

bat_hp_seasonal_pc, cos_hp_seasonal_sw_pc, cos_hp_seasonal_by_util_pc = (
    load_scenario_proposed_cos(SCENARIOS[0])
)
print(
    f"HP seasonal (percustomer) loaded: {bat_hp_seasonal_pc.height:,} buildings, "
    f"weighted customers: {bat_hp_seasonal_pc['weight'].sum():,.0f}"
)
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-hp-seasonal-sw-totals-pc
#| tbl-cap: "Default vs HP seasonal electric delivery revenue, electric delivery cost of service, and cross-subsidy — NY statewide (per-customer residual)"

_sc = SCENARIOS[0]
_spanner_today = f"All customers on utility's default rates"
_spanner_proposed = (
    f"HP customers on {_sc['hp_tariff'].replace('_', ' ')} rate; "
    f"non-HP customers on adjusted default rate"
)
display_gt(
    build_gt_cos_comparison_totals(
        cos_default_sw_pc,
        cos_hp_seasonal_sw_pc,
        title="Heat pump rate eliminates cross-subsidy from electric customers with heat pumps to those who heat with fossil fuels",
        label_spanner_today=_spanner_today,
        label_spanner_proposed=_spanner_proposed,
    )
)
```
