## HP seasonal vs. baseline statewide — averages (per-customer residual)

```{python}
#| label: tbl-hp-seasonal-sw-avg-pc
#| tbl-cap: "Average electric delivery bill, delivery cost of service, and cross-subsidy by heating fuel type, under two sections: 1. all customers under each utility's current default rates and 2. all heat pump customers on utility's HP seasonal rate and all other customers on utility's adjusted default rate. Assumes lump-sum residual allocation (equal residual share for all customers)."

display_gt(
    build_gt_cos_comparison_avg(
        cos_default_sw_pc,
        cos_hp_seasonal_sw_pc,
        title="Heat pump rate eliminates cross-subsidy from electric customers with heat pumps to those who heat with fossil fuels",
        label_spanner_default=_spanner_today,
        label_spanner_proposed=_spanner_proposed,
    )
)
```

## EH seasonal vs. baseline statewide — totals (per-customer residual)

```{python}
#| label: load-eh-seasonal-pc

bat_eh_seasonal_pc, cos_eh_seasonal_sw_pc, cos_eh_seasonal_by_util_pc = (
    load_scenario_proposed_cos(SCENARIOS[4])
)
print(
    f"EH seasonal (percustomer) loaded: {bat_eh_seasonal_pc.height:,} buildings, "
    f"weighted customers: {bat_eh_seasonal_pc['weight'].sum():,.0f}"
)
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-eh-seasonal-sw-totals-pc
#| tbl-cap: "Default vs EH seasonal delivery revenue, COS, and cross-subsidy — NY statewide (per-customer residual). The EH seasonal rate applies to HP and ER customers jointly."

_spanner_today_eh = "All customers on utility's default rates"
_spanner_proposed_eh = (
    "Electric-heating customers (HP + ER) on EH seasonal rate; "
    "fossil-fuel customers on adjusted flat rate"
)
display_gt(
    build_gt_cos_comparison_totals(
        cos_default_sw_pc,
        cos_eh_seasonal_sw_pc,
        title="EH seasonal rate eliminates cross-subsidy from electric-heating customers to those who heat with fossil fuels",
        label_spanner_today=_spanner_today_eh,
        label_spanner_proposed=_spanner_proposed_eh,
    )
)
```

## EH seasonal vs. baseline statewide — averages (per-customer residual)

```{python}
#| label: tbl-eh-seasonal-sw-avg-pc
#| tbl-cap: "Average electric delivery bill, delivery cost of service, and cross-subsidy by subclass, under two sections: 1. all customers under each utility's current default rates and 2. all electric-heating customers (HP + ER) on utility's EH seasonal rate and all other customers on utility's adjusted flat rate. Assumes lump-sum residual allocation (equal residual share for all customers)."

display_gt(
    build_gt_cos_comparison_avg(
        cos_default_sw_pc,
        cos_eh_seasonal_sw_pc,
        title="EH seasonal rate eliminates cross-subsidy from electric-heating customers to those who heat with fossil fuels",
        label_spanner_default=_spanner_today_eh,
        label_spanner_proposed=_spanner_proposed_eh,
    )
)
```

## HP TOU flex vs. baseline statewide — totals (per-customer residual)

```{python}
#| label: load-hp-tou-flex-pc

bat_tou_flex_pc, cos_tou_flex_sw_pc, cos_tou_flex_by_util_pc = (
    load_scenario_proposed_cos(SCENARIOS[2])
)
print(
    f"HP TOU flex (percustomer) loaded: {bat_tou_flex_pc.height:,} buildings, "
    f"weighted customers: {bat_tou_flex_pc['weight'].sum():,.0f}"
)
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-hp-tou-flex-sw-totals-pc
#| tbl-cap: "Default vs HP TOU flex delivery revenue, COS, and cross-subsidy — NY statewide (per-customer residual)"

_sc = SCENARIOS[2]
_spanner_proposed = (
    f"HP customers on {_sc['hp_tariff'].replace('_', ' ')} rate; "
    f"non-HP customers on adjusted default rate"
)
display_gt(
    build_gt_cos_comparison_totals(
        cos_default_sw_pc,
        cos_tou_flex_sw_pc,
        title="Heat pump rate eliminates cross-subsidy from electric customers with heat pumps to those who heat with fossil fuels",
        label_spanner_today=_spanner_today,
        label_spanner_proposed=_spanner_proposed,
    )
)
```

## HP TOU flex vs. baseline statewide — averages (per-customer residual)

```{python}
#| label: tbl-hp-tou-flex-sw-avg-pc
#| tbl-cap: "Default vs HP TOU flex average electric delivery bill and cross-subsidy — NY statewide (per-customer residual)"

display_gt(
    build_gt_cos_comparison_avg(
        cos_default_sw_pc,
        cos_tou_flex_sw_pc,
        title="Heat pump rate eliminates cross-subsidy from electric customers with heat pumps to those who heat with fossil fuels",
        label_spanner_default=_spanner_today,
        label_spanner_proposed=_spanner_proposed,
    )
)
```

## Baseline per-utility — totals and averages (per-customer residual)

Default-rate tables for each of the seven NY utilities, using per-customer residual allocation. Each utility shows a totals table followed by an averages table.

```{python}
#| label: tbl-baseline-per-util-pc-totals-helper

def _cos_totals_for(util: str):
    _cos_u = cos_default_by_util_pc[util]
    return build_gt_cos_totals(
        _cos_u,
        title=cos_baseline_totals_title(UTIL_DISPLAY[util], _cos_u),
        source_note=cos_table_baseline_source_note(),
    )

_PER_UTIL_TOTALS_CAP_PREFIX = (
    "Total electric delivery revenue, electric delivery cost of service, and "
    "cross-subsidy by residential customer heating source subclass under the "
    "default rate, for"
)
_PER_UTIL_TOTALS_CAP_SUFFIX = (
    ". Assumes lump-sum (per-customer) residual allocation — equal residual "
    "share for all customers. Subclass cost-of-service calculated using NREL's "
    "CAIRO model."
)
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-baseline-per-util-pc-totals-cenhud
#| tbl-cap: "Total electric delivery revenue, electric delivery cost of service, and cross-subsidy by residential customer heating source subclass under the default rate, for Central Hudson. Assumes lump-sum (per-customer) residual allocation — equal residual share for all customers. Subclass cost-of-service calculated using NREL's CAIRO model."

display_gt(_cos_totals_for("cenhud"))
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-baseline-per-util-pc-totals-coned
#| tbl-cap: "Total electric delivery revenue, electric delivery cost of service, and cross-subsidy by residential customer heating source subclass under the default rate, for Con Edison. Assumes lump-sum (per-customer) residual allocation — equal residual share for all customers. Subclass cost-of-service calculated using NREL's CAIRO model."

display_gt(_cos_totals_for("coned"))
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-baseline-per-util-pc-totals-nimo
#| tbl-cap: "Total electric delivery revenue, electric delivery cost of service, and cross-subsidy by residential customer heating source subclass under the default rate, for National Grid. Assumes lump-sum (per-customer) residual allocation — equal residual share for all customers. Subclass cost-of-service calculated using NREL's CAIRO model."

display_gt(_cos_totals_for("nimo"))
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-baseline-per-util-pc-totals-nyseg
#| tbl-cap: "Total electric delivery revenue, electric delivery cost of service, and cross-subsidy by residential customer heating source subclass under the default rate, for NYSEG. Assumes lump-sum (per-customer) residual allocation — equal residual share for all customers. Subclass cost-of-service calculated using NREL's CAIRO model."

display_gt(_cos_totals_for("nyseg"))
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-baseline-per-util-pc-totals-or
#| tbl-cap: "Total electric delivery revenue, electric delivery cost of service, and cross-subsidy by residential customer heating source subclass under the default rate, for O&R. Assumes lump-sum (per-customer) residual allocation — equal residual share for all customers. Subclass cost-of-service calculated using NREL's CAIRO model."

display_gt(_cos_totals_for("or"))
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-baseline-per-util-pc-totals-psegli
#| tbl-cap: "Total electric delivery revenue, electric delivery cost of service, and cross-subsidy by residential customer heating source subclass under the default rate, for PSEG-LI. Assumes lump-sum (per-customer) residual allocation — equal residual share for all customers. Subclass cost-of-service calculated using NREL's CAIRO model."

display_gt(_cos_totals_for("psegli"))
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-baseline-per-util-pc-totals-rge
#| tbl-cap: "Total electric delivery revenue, electric delivery cost of service, and cross-subsidy by residential customer heating source subclass under the default rate, for RG&E. Assumes lump-sum (per-customer) residual allocation — equal residual share for all customers. Subclass cost-of-service calculated using NREL's CAIRO model."

display_gt(_cos_totals_for("rge"))
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-baseline-per-util-pc-avg-helper

def _cos_avg_for(util: str):
    _cos_u = cos_default_by_util_pc[util]
    return build_gt_cos_avg(
        _cos_u,
        title=cos_baseline_avg_title(UTIL_DISPLAY[util], _cos_u),
        source_note=cos_table_baseline_source_note(),
    )
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-baseline-per-util-pc-avg-cenhud
#| tbl-cap: "Average annual electric delivery bill, electric delivery cost of service, and cross-subsidy by residential customer heating source subclass under the default rate, for Central Hudson. Assumes lump-sum (per-customer) residual allocation — equal residual share for all customers."

display_gt(_cos_avg_for("cenhud"))
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-baseline-per-util-pc-avg-coned
#| tbl-cap: "Average annual electric delivery bill, electric delivery cost of service, and cross-subsidy by residential customer heating source subclass under the default rate, for Con Edison. Assumes lump-sum (per-customer) residual allocation — equal residual share for all customers."

display_gt(_cos_avg_for("coned"))
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-baseline-per-util-pc-avg-nimo
#| tbl-cap: "Average annual electric delivery bill, electric delivery cost of service, and cross-subsidy by residential customer heating source subclass under the default rate, for National Grid. Assumes lump-sum (per-customer) residual allocation — equal residual share for all customers."

display_gt(_cos_avg_for("nimo"))
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-baseline-per-util-pc-avg-nyseg
#| tbl-cap: "Average annual electric delivery bill, electric delivery cost of service, and cross-subsidy by residential customer heating source subclass under the default rate, for NYSEG. Assumes lump-sum (per-customer) residual allocation — equal residual share for all customers."

display_gt(_cos_avg_for("nyseg"))
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-baseline-per-util-pc-avg-or
#| tbl-cap: "Average annual electric delivery bill, electric delivery cost of service, and cross-subsidy by residential customer heating source subclass under the default rate, for O&R. Assumes lump-sum (per-customer) residual allocation — equal residual share for all customers."

display_gt(_cos_avg_for("or"))
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-baseline-per-util-pc-avg-psegli
#| tbl-cap: "Average annual electric delivery bill, electric delivery cost of service, and cross-subsidy by residential customer heating source subclass under the default rate, for PSEG-LI. Assumes lump-sum (per-customer) residual allocation — equal residual share for all customers."

display_gt(_cos_avg_for("psegli"))
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-baseline-per-util-pc-avg-rge
#| tbl-cap: "Average annual electric delivery bill, electric delivery cost of service, and cross-subsidy by residential customer heating source subclass under the default rate, for RG&E. Assumes lump-sum (per-customer) residual allocation — equal residual share for all customers."

display_gt(_cos_avg_for("rge"))
```


## HP seasonal vs. baseline per-utility — totals and averages (per-customer residual)

Comparison tables for each utility: default rates vs. HP seasonal rate (run 5+6), with per-customer residual allocation.

```{python}
#| label: hp-seasonal-per-util-pc

_sc = SCENARIOS[0]
for util in UTILITIES:
    _label_u = UTIL_DISPLAY[util]
    _c_hp = calibrated_volumetric_c_per_kwh(util, _sc["hp_tariff"])
    _c_nonhp = calibrated_volumetric_c_per_kwh(util, _sc["nonhp_tariff"])
    _c_def = calibrated_volumetric_c_per_kwh(util, "default")
    _span_today = f"Default rates ({_c_def})"
    _span_prop = (
        f"HP {_sc['hp_tariff'].replace('_', ' ')} ({_c_hp}); "
        f"non-HP {_sc['nonhp_tariff'].replace('_', ' ')} ({_c_nonhp})"
    )
    display_gt(
        build_gt_cos_comparison_totals(
            cos_default_by_util_pc[util],
            cos_hp_seasonal_by_util_pc[util],
            title=f"Delivery revenue, COS, and cross-subsidy — {_label_u}",
            label_spanner_today=_span_today,
            label_spanner_proposed=_span_prop,
        )
    )
    display_gt(
        build_gt_cos_comparison_avg(
            cos_default_by_util_pc[util],
            cos_hp_seasonal_by_util_pc[util],
            title=f"Average electric delivery bill, COS, and cross-subsidy — {_label_u}",
            label_spanner_default=_span_today,
            label_spanner_proposed=_span_prop,
        )
    )
print("Displayed HP seasonal per-utility comparison tables (percustomer)")
```

## HP TOU flex vs. baseline per-utility — totals and averages (per-customer residual)

Comparison tables for each utility: default rates vs. HP seasonal TOU flex rate (run 13+14), with per-customer residual allocation.

```{python}
#| label: hp-tou-flex-per-util-pc

_sc = SCENARIOS[2]
for util in UTILITIES:
    _label_u = UTIL_DISPLAY[util]
    _c_hp = calibrated_volumetric_c_per_kwh(util, _sc["hp_tariff"])
    _c_nonhp = calibrated_volumetric_c_per_kwh(util, _sc["nonhp_tariff"])
    _c_def = calibrated_volumetric_c_per_kwh(util, "default")
    _span_today = f"Default rates ({_c_def})"
    _span_prop = (
        f"HP {_sc['hp_tariff'].replace('_', ' ')} ({_c_hp}); "
        f"non-HP {_sc['nonhp_tariff'].replace('_', ' ')} ({_c_nonhp})"
    )
    display_gt(
        build_gt_cos_comparison_totals(
            cos_default_by_util_pc[util],
            cos_tou_flex_by_util_pc[util],
            title=f"Delivery revenue, COS, and cross-subsidy — {_label_u}",
            label_spanner_today=_span_today,
            label_spanner_proposed=_span_prop,
        )
    )
    display_gt(
        build_gt_cos_comparison_avg(
            cos_default_by_util_pc[util],
            cos_tou_flex_by_util_pc[util],
            title=f"Average delivery bill, COS, and cross-subsidy — {_label_u}",
            label_spanner_default=_span_today,
            label_spanner_proposed=_span_prop,
        )
    )
print("Displayed HP TOU flex per-utility comparison tables (percustomer)")
```

## Report variables (per-customer residual)

```{python}
#| label: report-vars-pc

fill_report_vars_for_scenario(
    SCENARIOS[0]["id"],
    cos_hp_seasonal_sw_pc,
    cos_hp_seasonal_by_util_pc,
)
fill_report_vars_for_scenario(
    SCENARIOS[2]["id"],
    cos_tou_flex_sw_pc,
    cos_tou_flex_by_util_pc,
)
fill_report_vars_for_scenario(
    SCENARIOS[4]["id"],
    cos_eh_seasonal_sw_pc,
    cos_eh_seasonal_by_util_pc,
)
print("Per-customer report vars saved for HP seasonal, HP TOU flex, and EH seasonal")
```

---

# EPMC DELIVERY RESIDUAL, PER-CUSTOMER SUPPLY RESIDUAL {#sec-epmc}

All tables in this section use **EPMC residual allocation** for delivery and **per-customer** for supply. The baseline is run 1+2 (default uniform rates) with EPMC-allocated COS columns; the proposed scenarios are HP seasonal EPMC (run 21+22) and HP seasonal TOU flex EPMC (run 25+26).

## Load baseline data (EPMC residual)

The baseline _revenue_ is the same as above (run 1+2, same bills), but the COS decomposition uses the EPMC residual allocation columns instead of per-customer.

```{python}
#| label: load-default-epmc
#| eval: false

bat_default_epmc = load_master_bat(
    PATH_MASTER_BAT_DEFAULT, residual_alloc="epmc"
)
bat_default_epmc = join_resstock_annual_elec_kwh(
    bat_default_epmc,
    path_state_monthly=PATH_RESSTOCK_NY_LOAD_CURVE_MONTHLY,
    run_pair=RUN_DEFAULT,
    path_cache_dir=PATH_COS_NOTEBOOK_CACHE,
)
assert_bill_identity(bat_default_epmc, "run 1+2 (default, epmc)")

cos_default_sw_epmc = build_cos_table(bat_default_epmc, rev_req=statewide_delivery_rr)

cos_default_by_util_epmc: dict[str, pl.DataFrame] = {}
for util in UTILITIES:
    cos_default_by_util_epmc[util] = build_cos_table(
        bat_default_epmc, rev_req=rev_req[util]["total_delivery_rr"], utility_filter=util
    )

print(f"Default BAT (EPMC) loaded: {bat_default_epmc.height:,} buildings")
print(f"  Weighted customer count: {bat_default_epmc['weight'].sum():,.0f}")
print(f"  Weighted delivery revenue: ${(bat_default_epmc['weight'] * bat_default_epmc['annual_bill_delivery']).sum():,.0f}")
```

## Baseline statewide — summary (EPMC residual)

```{python}
#| label: tbl-baseline-sw-summary-epmc
#| eval: false
#| tbl-cap: "Residential customers, consumption, and delivery revenue by heating fuel type — NY statewide, default rates (EPMC residual)."

display_gt(
    build_gt_cos_totals_abridged(
        cos_default_sw_epmc,
        title=cos_sw_summary_abridged_title(cos_default_sw_epmc),
    )
)
```

## Baseline statewide — totals (EPMC residual)

```{python}
#| label: tbl-baseline-sw-totals-epmc
#| eval: false
#| tbl-cap: "Total electric delivery revenue, electric delivery cost of service, and cross-subsidy by residential customer heating fuel type, under default rates. Assumes equal-percentage-of-marginal-cost (EPMC) residual allocation: each subclass is allocated a share of the residual in proportion to its share of total marginal cost."

display_gt(
    build_gt_cos_totals(
        cos_default_sw_epmc,
        title=cos_baseline_totals_title(None, cos_default_sw_epmc),
        source_note=cos_table_baseline_source_note(residual="epmc"),
        statewide_narrow=True,
    )
)
```

## Baseline statewide — averages (EPMC residual)

```{python}
#| label: tbl-baseline-sw-avg-epmc
#| eval: false
#| tbl-cap: "Average annual electric delivery bill, electric delivery cost of service, and cross-subsidy by residential customer subclass, under default rates. Assumes equal-percentage-of-marginal-cost (EPMC) residual allocation: each subclass is allocated a share of the residual in proportion to its share of total marginal cost."

display_gt(
    build_gt_cos_avg(
        cos_default_sw_epmc,
        title=cos_baseline_avg_title(None, cos_default_sw_epmc),
        source_note=cos_table_baseline_source_note(residual="epmc"),
    )
)
```

## Baseline statewide — marginal cost diagnostic (EPMC residual)

Same decomposition as the per-customer diagnostic above. The **marginal cost** column should be identical to the per-customer table (MC does not depend on residual allocation). The **residual** column reflects EPMC allocation, which distributes the residual in proportion to each subclass's marginal cost share rather than uniformly per customer.

```{python}
#| label: tbl-mc-diagnostic-sw-epmc
#| eval: false
#| tbl-cap: "Marginal cost, residual, and COS decomposition by subclass — NY statewide (EPMC residual)"

display_gt(
    build_gt_mc_diagnostic(
        bat_default_epmc,
        title="Marginal cost, residual, and COS decomposition — NY statewide",
    )
)
```

## HP seasonal vs. baseline statewide — totals (EPMC residual)

```{python}
#| label: load-hp-seasonal-epmc
#| eval: false

bat_hp_seasonal_epmc, cos_hp_seasonal_sw_epmc, cos_hp_seasonal_by_util_epmc = (
    load_scenario_proposed_cos(SCENARIOS[1])
)
print(
    f"HP seasonal (EPMC) loaded: {bat_hp_seasonal_epmc.height:,} buildings, "
    f"weighted customers: {bat_hp_seasonal_epmc['weight'].sum():,.0f}"
)
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-hp-seasonal-sw-totals-epmc
#| eval: false
#| tbl-cap: "Default vs HP seasonal delivery revenue, COS, and cross-subsidy — NY statewide (EPMC residual)"

_sc = SCENARIOS[1]
_spanner_proposed = (
    f"HP customers on {_sc['hp_tariff'].replace('_', ' ')} rate; "
    f"non-HP customers on adjusted default rate"
)
display_gt(
    build_gt_cos_comparison_totals(
        cos_default_sw_epmc,
        cos_hp_seasonal_sw_epmc,
        title="Heat pump rate eliminates cross-subsidy from electric customers with heat pumps to those who heat with fossil fuels",
        label_spanner_today=_spanner_today,
        label_spanner_proposed=_spanner_proposed,
    )
)
```

## HP seasonal vs. baseline statewide — averages (EPMC residual)

```{python}
#| label: tbl-hp-seasonal-sw-avg-epmc
#| eval: false
#| tbl-cap: "Default vs HP seasonal average delivery bill and cross-subsidy — NY statewide (EPMC residual)"

display_gt(
    build_gt_cos_comparison_avg(
        cos_default_sw_epmc,
        cos_hp_seasonal_sw_epmc,
        title="Heat pump rate eliminates cross-subsidy from electric customers with heat pumps to those who heat with fossil fuels",
        label_spanner_default=_spanner_today,
        label_spanner_proposed=_spanner_proposed,
    )
)
```

## HP TOU flex vs. baseline statewide — totals (EPMC residual)

Disabled: the current batch (`ny_20260416a_r1-36`) does not include run pair
25+26 (HP TOU flex EPMC). Re-enable when a batch with those runs is wired up.

```{python}
#| label: load-hp-tou-flex-epmc
#| eval: false

bat_tou_flex_epmc, cos_tou_flex_sw_epmc, cos_tou_flex_by_util_epmc = (
    load_scenario_proposed_cos(SCENARIOS[3])
)
print(
    f"HP TOU flex (EPMC) loaded: {bat_tou_flex_epmc.height:,} buildings, "
    f"weighted customers: {bat_tou_flex_epmc['weight'].sum():,.0f}"
)
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-hp-tou-flex-sw-totals-epmc
#| tbl-cap: "Default vs HP TOU flex delivery revenue, COS, and cross-subsidy — NY statewide (EPMC residual)"
#| eval: false

_sc = SCENARIOS[3]
_spanner_proposed = (
    f"HP customers on {_sc['hp_tariff'].replace('_', ' ')} rate; "
    f"non-HP customers on adjusted default rate"
)
display_gt(
    build_gt_cos_comparison_totals(
        cos_default_sw_epmc,
        cos_tou_flex_sw_epmc,
        title="Heat pump rate eliminates cross-subsidy from electric customers with heat pumps to those who heat with fossil fuels",
        label_spanner_today=_spanner_today,
        label_spanner_proposed=_spanner_proposed,
    )
)
```

## HP TOU flex vs. baseline statewide — averages (EPMC residual)

```{python}
#| label: tbl-hp-tou-flex-sw-avg-epmc
#| tbl-cap: "Default vs HP TOU flex average delivery bill and cross-subsidy — NY statewide (EPMC residual)"
#| eval: false

display_gt(
    build_gt_cos_comparison_avg(
        cos_default_sw_epmc,
        cos_tou_flex_sw_epmc,
        title="Heat pump rate eliminates cross-subsidy from electric customers with heat pumps to those who heat with fossil fuels",
        label_spanner_default=_spanner_today,
        label_spanner_proposed=_spanner_proposed,
    )
)
```

## Baseline per-utility — totals and averages (EPMC residual)

Default-rate tables for each of the seven NY utilities, using EPMC residual allocation. Each utility shows a totals table followed by an averages table.

```{python}
#| label: baseline-per-util-epmc
#| eval: false

for util in UTILITIES:
    _label_u = UTIL_DISPLAY[util]
    _cos_u = cos_default_by_util_epmc[util]
    display_gt(
        build_gt_cos_totals(
            _cos_u,
            title=cos_baseline_totals_title(_label_u, _cos_u),
            source_note=cos_table_baseline_source_note(residual="epmc"),
        )
    )
    display_gt(
        build_gt_cos_avg(
            _cos_u,
            title=cos_baseline_avg_title(_label_u, _cos_u),
            source_note=cos_table_baseline_source_note(residual="epmc"),
        )
    )
print("Displayed baseline per-utility tables (EPMC)")
```

## HP seasonal vs. baseline per-utility — totals and averages (EPMC residual)

Comparison tables for each utility: default rates vs. HP seasonal rate (run 21+22), with EPMC residual allocation.

```{python}
#| label: hp-seasonal-per-util-epmc
#| eval: false

_sc = SCENARIOS[1]
for util in UTILITIES:
    _label_u = UTIL_DISPLAY[util]
    _c_hp = calibrated_volumetric_c_per_kwh(util, _sc["hp_tariff"])
    _c_nonhp = calibrated_volumetric_c_per_kwh(util, _sc["nonhp_tariff"])
    _c_def = calibrated_volumetric_c_per_kwh(util, "default")
    _span_today = f"Default rates ({_c_def})"
    _span_prop = (
        f"HP {_sc['hp_tariff'].replace('_', ' ')} ({_c_hp}); "
        f"non-HP {_sc['nonhp_tariff'].replace('_', ' ')} ({_c_nonhp})"
    )
    display_gt(
        build_gt_cos_comparison_totals(
            cos_default_by_util_epmc[util],
            cos_hp_seasonal_by_util_epmc[util],
            title=f"Delivery revenue, COS, and cross-subsidy — {_label_u}",
            label_spanner_today=_span_today,
            label_spanner_proposed=_span_prop,
        )
    )
    display_gt(
        build_gt_cos_comparison_avg(
            cos_default_by_util_epmc[util],
            cos_hp_seasonal_by_util_epmc[util],
            title=f"Average delivery bill, COS, and cross-subsidy — {_label_u}",
            label_spanner_default=_span_today,
            label_spanner_proposed=_span_prop,
        )
    )
print("Displayed HP seasonal per-utility comparison tables (EPMC)")
```

## HP TOU flex vs. baseline per-utility — totals and averages (EPMC residual)

Comparison tables for each utility: default rates vs. HP seasonal TOU flex rate (run 25+26), with EPMC residual allocation.

Disabled: the current batch (`ny_20260416a_r1-36`) does not include run pair
25+26. Re-enable when a batch with those runs is wired up.

```{python}
#| label: hp-tou-flex-per-util-epmc
#| eval: false

_sc = SCENARIOS[3]
for util in UTILITIES:
    _label_u = UTIL_DISPLAY[util]
    _c_hp = calibrated_volumetric_c_per_kwh(util, _sc["hp_tariff"])
    _c_nonhp = calibrated_volumetric_c_per_kwh(util, _sc["nonhp_tariff"])
    _c_def = calibrated_volumetric_c_per_kwh(util, "default")
    _span_today = f"Default rates ({_c_def})"
    _span_prop = (
        f"HP {_sc['hp_tariff'].replace('_', ' ')} ({_c_hp}); "
        f"non-HP {_sc['nonhp_tariff'].replace('_', ' ')} ({_c_nonhp})"
    )
    display_gt(
        build_gt_cos_comparison_totals(
            cos_default_by_util_epmc[util],
            cos_tou_flex_by_util_epmc[util],
            title=f"Delivery revenue, COS, and cross-subsidy — {_label_u}",
            label_spanner_today=_span_today,
            label_spanner_proposed=_span_prop,
        )
    )
    display_gt(
        build_gt_cos_comparison_avg(
            cos_default_by_util_epmc[util],
            cos_tou_flex_by_util_epmc[util],
            title=f"Average delivery bill, COS, and cross-subsidy — {_label_u}",
            label_spanner_default=_span_today,
            label_spanner_proposed=_span_prop,
        )
    )
print("Displayed HP TOU flex per-utility comparison tables (EPMC)")
```

## Report variables (EPMC residual)

```{python}
#| label: report-vars-epmc
#| eval: false

fill_report_vars_for_scenario(
    SCENARIOS[1]["id"],
    cos_hp_seasonal_sw_epmc,
    cos_hp_seasonal_by_util_epmc,
)
# SCENARIOS[3] (HP TOU flex EPMC, run 25+26) disabled — batch
# ``ny_20260416a_r1-36`` does not include those runs. Re-enable alongside
# the disabled TOU flex EPMC table cells above when a batch with 25+26 is
# wired up.
# fill_report_vars_for_scenario(
#     SCENARIOS[3]["id"],
#     cos_tou_flex_sw_epmc,
#     cos_tou_flex_by_util_epmc,
# )
print("EPMC report vars saved for HP seasonal (HP TOU flex disabled)")
```

---

# Default-rate report variables

```{python}
#| label: default-report-vars

_hp_default_sw = cos_default_sw_pc.filter(pl.col("subclass") == "Heat pump")
_all_default_sw = cos_default_sw_pc.filter(pl.col("subclass") == "All customers")

report_vars["default"] = {
    "statewide": {
        "total_customers": float(_all_default_sw["n_customers"][0]),
        "total_delivery_rev": float(_all_default_sw["revenue_delivery"][0]),
        "hp_cross_subsidy": float(_hp_default_sw["cross_subsidy"][0]),
        "hp_pct_overpayment": float(_hp_default_sw["pct_overpayment_vs_cos"][0])
        if _hp_default_sw["pct_overpayment_vs_cos"][0] is not None
        else None,
    },
    "by_utility": {},
}

for util in UTILITIES:
    _cos_d = cos_default_by_util_pc[util]
    _hp_d = _cos_d.filter(pl.col("subclass") == "Heat pump")
    report_vars["default"]["by_utility"][util] = {
        "hp_cross_subsidy": float(_hp_d["cross_subsidy"][0]),
        "hp_pct_overpayment": float(_hp_d["pct_overpayment_vs_cos"][0])
        if _hp_d["pct_overpayment_vs_cos"][0] is not None
        else None,
    }

report_vars["statewide_delivery_rr"] = statewide_delivery_rr
report_vars["statewide_supply_rr"] = statewide_supply_rr
report_vars["statewide_residential_kwh"] = statewide_residential_kwh
report_vars["rev_req_by_utility"] = rev_req

print("Default statewide HP cross-subsidy:", f"${report_vars['default']['statewide']['hp_cross_subsidy']:,.0f}")
for util in UTILITIES:
    _xs = report_vars["default"]["by_utility"][util]["hp_cross_subsidy"]
    print(f"  {UTIL_DISPLAY[util]:20s} HP cross-subsidy: ${_xs:,.0f}")
```

# Export report variables

```{python}
#| label: export-report-vars

import pickle


def _avg_cross_subsidy_per_customer(cos_tbl: pl.DataFrame, subclass: str) -> float:
    """Weighted average BAT delivery cross-subsidy per customer (matches ``build_gt_cos_comparison_avg``)."""
    row = cos_tbl.filter(pl.col("subclass") == subclass)
    if row.is_empty():
        return float("nan")
    nc = float(row["n_customers"][0])
    if nc <= 0:
        return float("nan")
    return float(row["cross_subsidy"][0]) / nc


def _avg_kwh_per_customer(cos_tbl: pl.DataFrame, subclass: str) -> float:
    """Weighted mean annual kWh per customer (``kwh_total`` / ``n_customers`` for the subclass row)."""
    row = cos_tbl.filter(pl.col("subclass") == subclass)
    if row.is_empty():
        return float("nan")
    nc = float(row["n_customers"][0])
    if nc <= 0:
        return float("nan")
    return float(row["kwh_total"][0]) / nc


# Statewide per-customer residual: default (1+2) vs HP seasonal proposed (5+6), for narrative in ``index.qmd``.
report_vars["natgas_avg_xsub_default_pc"] = _avg_cross_subsidy_per_customer(
    cos_default_sw_pc, "Natural gas"
)
report_vars["natgas_avg_xsub_hp_seasonal_pc"] = _avg_cross_subsidy_per_customer(
    cos_hp_seasonal_sw_pc, "Natural gas"
)
report_vars["delivered_fuels_avg_xsub_default_pc"] = _avg_cross_subsidy_per_customer(
    cos_default_sw_pc, "Delivered fuels"
)
report_vars["delivered_fuels_avg_xsub_hp_seasonal_pc"] = _avg_cross_subsidy_per_customer(
    cos_hp_seasonal_sw_pc, "Delivered fuels"
)

_hp_sw = cos_default_sw_pc.filter(pl.col("subclass") == "Heat pump")
if _hp_sw.height != 1:
    msg = "Expected exactly one 'Heat pump' row for statewide default COS"
    raise ValueError(msg)
report_vars["hp_share_of_delivery_revenue"] = float(_hp_sw["pct_all_revenue"][0])
_kwh_hp = _avg_kwh_per_customer(cos_default_sw_pc, "Heat pump")
_kwh_natgas = _avg_kwh_per_customer(cos_default_sw_pc, "Natural gas")
report_vars["hp_avg_kwh_over_natgas_avg_kwh"] = (
    _kwh_hp / _kwh_natgas if _kwh_natgas > 0 else float("nan")
)

Path("../cache").mkdir(exist_ok=True)
Path("../cache/report_variables_cos_subclass.pkl").write_bytes(
    pickle.dumps(report_vars),
)
print(f"Exported {len(report_vars)} top-level keys to cache/report_variables_cos_subclass.pkl")
```
